2. Add spinach and cook until wilted, about 1-2 minutes. Remove from pan and set aside.
3. Whisk eggs with salt and pepper in a bowl.
4. Pour eggs into the same pan. Cook, stirring gently, until eggs are set but still moist.
5. Fold in the cooked spinach.
6. Toast the wholemeal bread and serve with the scrambled eggs.

lunch.name: Halal Grilled Chicken Breast with Quinoa Salad
lunch.description: Lean grilled chicken breast served with a vibrant quinoa salad featuring mixed vegetables.
instructions: 1. Season chicken breast with salt and pepper. Grill or pan-fry until cooked through (internal temperature 74C), about 6-8 minutes per side.
2. In a bowl, combine cooked quinoa, diced cucumber, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and fresh parsley.
3. Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper to make a dressing. Pour over the quinoa salad and toss to combine.
4. Slice the grilled chicken breast and serve alongside the quinoa salad.

dinner.name: Halal Lean Beef Stir-fry with Brown Rice
dinner.description: Quick and flavorful stir-fry made with lean Halal beef, colorful vegetables, and served with brown rice.
instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a large wok or pan over high heat.
2. Add beef strips and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until browned. Remove beef and set aside.
3. Add broccoli, bell pepper, and carrots to the pan. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until vegetables are crisp-tender.
4. Add minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
5. Return beef to the pan. Add Halal soy sauce and sesame oil. Toss to combine and heat through.
6. Serve the stir-fry immediately with cooked brown rice.

snack.name: Greek Yogurt with Berries
snack.description: Creamy Greek yogurt topped with a handful of fresh mixed berries.
instructions: 1. Place Greek yogurt in a bowl.
2. Top with fresh mixed berries and enjoy.

breakfast.name: Overnight Oats with Chia Seeds and Apple
breakfast.description: A convenient and nutritious breakfast prepared the night before, featuring oats, chia seeds, and apple.
ingredients: 150ml unsweetened almond milk (or other Halal plant-based milk)
instructions: 1. In a jar or container, combine rolled oats, chia seeds, and almond milk.
2. Stir well. Add grated or diced apple and stir again.
3. Cover and refrigerate overnight (or for at least 4 hours).
4. In the morning, stir and drizzle with honey if desired before eating.

lunch.name: Tuna Salad Lettuce Wraps
lunch.description: Light and refreshing tuna salad served in crisp lettuce cups instead of bread.
ingredients: 2 large lettuce leaves (e.g., butter lettuce or romaine)
instructions: 1. In a bowl, flake the drained tuna.
2. Add light mayonnaise, diced celery, diced red onion, salt, and pepper. Mix well to combine.
3. Spoon the tuna salad into the lettuce leaves.
4. Fold or roll the lettuce leaves to create wraps.

dinner.name: Halal Baked Salmon with Asparagus and Sweet Potato
dinner.description: A balanced meal featuring oven-baked salmon, tender asparagus, and a side of roasted sweet potato.
instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (390°F).
2. On a baking tray, toss sweet potato cubes with 5ml olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast for 15 minutes.

3. Add asparagus to the tray with the sweet potato, toss with a little olive oil. Place salmon fillet on the same tray. Drizzle salmon with remaining 5ml olive oil, salt, pepper, and fresh dill if using.
4. Bake for another 15-20 minutes, or until salmon is cooked through and flakes easily, and vegetables are tender.

snack.name: Small handful of almonds
snack.description: A crunchy and satisfying snack providing healthy fats and protein.
instructions: 1. Measure out almonds and enjoy as a snack.

breakfast.name: High-Protein Smoothie
breakfast.description: A quick and energizing smoothie packed with protein and nutrients.
ingredients: 1 scoop (30g) Halal whey protein powder (vanilla or unflavored)
instructions: 1. Combine protein powder, almond milk, banana, spinach, and chia seeds in a blender.
2. Blend until smooth. Add more liquid if needed to reach desired consistency.
3. Pour into a glass and serve immediately.

lunch.name: Lentil Soup
lunch.description: Hearty and nutritious lentil soup, a great source of fiber and plant-based protein.
instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat.
2. Add onion, carrots, and celery. Sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened.
3. Add cooked lentils, vegetable broth, cumin, salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer.
4. Cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing flavors to meld. If too thick, add a little water or more broth.
5. Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley if desired.

dinner.name: Halal Turkey Mince Lettuce Cups
dinner.description: Savory Halal turkey mince seasoned and served in crisp lettuce cups for a light yet satisfying dinner.
ingredients: 2 large lettuce leaves (e.g., iceberg or butter lettuce)
instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
2. Add turkey mince and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned and cooked through. Drain any excess fat.
3. Add minced garlic and grated ginger, cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
4. Stir in Halal soy sauce, rice vinegar, chopped water chestnuts, and shredded carrots. Cook for another 2-3 minutes until heated through.
5. Spoon the turkey mixture into lettuce cups.
6. Garnish with fresh coriander if desired and serve.

snack.name: Rice cakes with peanut butter
snack.description: Crispy rice cakes topped with natural peanut butter for a satisfying crunch.
instructions: 1. Spread peanut butter evenly over the rice cakes.
2. Enjoy as a quick snack.

breakfast.name: Fruit and Cottage Cheese Bowl
breakfast.description: A refreshing bowl of cottage cheese paired with fresh fruit for a protein-rich start.
instructions: 1. Place cottage cheese in a bowl.
2. Top with mixed berries and chopped walnuts if using.

lunch.name: Chicken and Vegetable Skewers with Hummus
lunch.description: Grilled Halal chicken and colorful vegetables on skewers, served with a side of creamy hummus.
instructions: 1. Thread chicken cubes, bell pepper, zucchini, and red onion onto skewers.
2. Drizzle with olive oil and season with paprika, salt, and pepper.
3. Grill or pan-fry the skewers for 10-15 minutes, turning occasionally, until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender-crisp.
4. Serve the skewers with a side of hummus.

dinner.name: Shrimp (Prawn) and Broccoli Stir-fry
dinner.description: A quick and light stir-fry featuring succulent shrimp and crunchy broccoli.
ingredients: 50g cooked brown rice (optional, add extra ~170kcal for 100g cooked)
instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a wok or large pan over medium-high heat.
2. Add broccoli florets and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until bright green and slightly tender.
3. Add minced garlic and grated ginger, cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
4. Add shrimp and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until pink and cooked through.
5. Stir in Halal soy sauce and sesame oil. Toss everything to combine.
6. Serve hot, optionally with a small portion of cooked brown rice (not included in kcal calculation for this meal to stay within target).

snack.name: Hard-boiled egg
snack.description: A simple and convenient high-protein snack.
instructions: 1. Boil egg for 8-10 minutes until hard-boiled.
2. Peel and enjoy.

breakfast.name: Avocado Toast with Tomato
breakfast.description: Creamy avocado spread on wholemeal toast, topped with fresh tomato slices.
instructions: 1. Toast the wholemeal bread.
2. In a small bowl, mash the avocado with salt and pepper.
3. Spread the mashed avocado evenly over the toast.
4. Arrange tomato slices on top. Sprinkle with red pepper flakes if desired.

lunch.name: Chicken and Chickpea Salad
lunch.description: A fulfilling salad with shredded Halal chicken, chickpeas, and fresh vegetables in a light dressing.
instructions: 1. In a large bowl, combine shredded chicken, chickpeas, diced cucumber, diced bell pepper, and red onion.
2. In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, fresh parsley, salt, and pepper.
3. Pour the dressing over the salad ingredients and toss gently to coat.
4. Serve chilled.

dinner.name: Halal Lamb Tagine with Cauliflower Couscous
dinner.description: Flavorful Halal lamb cooked in a rich, aromatic tagine sauce, served with a low-carb cauliflower 'couscous'.
ingredients: 150g Halal lamb leg or shoulder, cubed
instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a pot or deep pan over medium-high heat. Brown lamb cubes on all sides. Remove lamb and set aside.
2. Add onion to the pot and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute more.
3. Return lamb to the pot. Stir in diced tomatoes, Halal broth, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and cinnamon.
4. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 60-90 minutes, or until lamb is tender. Stir occasionally.
5. While lamb is cooking, steam or microwave riced cauliflower until tender, about 3-5 minutes.
6. Serve the lamb tagine over cauliflower couscous, garnished with fresh mint if desired.

snack.name: Orange
snack.description: A juicy and refreshing citrus fruit.
instructions: 1. Peel and enjoy one medium orange.

breakfast.name: Cinnamon Raisin Oatmeal
breakfast.description: Warm and comforting oatmeal with a hint of cinnamon and sweet raisins.
instructions: 1. Combine oats, water/almond milk, and cinnamon in a small saucepan.
2. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce heat and simmer for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until oats are creamy and liquid is absorbed.
3. Stir in raisins. If desired, drizzle with honey before serving.

lunch.name: Halloumi and Vegetable Wrap
lunch.description: Grilled Halal halloumi cheese with fresh vegetables wrapped in a wholemeal tortilla.
instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat.
2. Add halloumi slices and cook for 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown.
3. Lay the tortilla flat. Arrange mixed greens, cucumber, bell pepper, and grilled halloumi slices in the center.
4. Sprinkle with dried oregano.
5. Fold in the sides of the tortilla, then roll up tightly to form a wrap.

dinner.name: Halal Chicken and Vegetable Curry
dinner.description: A mild and aromatic curry with tender Halal chicken and a variety of vegetables.
instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat.
2. Add onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and grated ginger, cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
3. Add chicken cubes and cook until lightly browned on all sides.
4. Stir in curry powder, salt, and pepper. Cook for 1 minute.
5. Pour in light coconut milk, add zucchini and green beans. Bring to a simmer.
6. Reduce heat and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender. Serve hot with cooked brown rice.

snack.name: Carrot sticks with a spoonful of hummus
snack.description: Crunchy carrot sticks paired with creamy hummus for a satisfying vegetable snack.
instructions: 1. Wash and cut carrots into sticks.
2. Serve with hummus for dipping.

breakfast.name: Berry Smoothie Bowl
breakfast.description: A thick, refreshing smoothie bowl topped with fresh berries and a sprinkle of seeds.
instructions: 1. In a blender, combine frozen berries, almond milk, and Greek yogurt. Blend until thick and smooth, adding more milk if needed.
2. Pour the smoothie into a bowl.
3. Top with chia seeds and shredded coconut if desired. Serve immediately.

lunch.name: Egg and Vegetable Frittata Slice
lunch.description: A protein-packed frittata slice loaded with colorful vegetables, perfect for meal prep.
instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F).
2. Heat olive oil in an oven-safe, non-stick pan (approx 15-20cm diameter) over medium heat.
3. Add onion and bell pepper; sauté for 3-5 minutes until softened. Add spinach and cook until wilted.
4. Whisk eggs with salt and pepper in a bowl.
5. Pour egg mixture over the vegetables in the pan. Cook on the stovetop for 2-3 minutes until the edges begin to set.
6. Transfer the pan to the preheated oven and bake for 10-15 minutes, or until the frittata is set and lightly golden. Cut into 1 portion.

dinner.name: Halal Ground Beef and Bean Chili
dinner.description: A hearty and flavorful chili made with lean Halal ground beef and kidney beans, packed with spices.
ingredients: 80g canned kidney beans, rinsed and drained
instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a pot or large pan over medium-high heat. Add ground beef and cook, breaking it up, until browned. Drain any excess fat.
2. Add onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
3. Stir in diced tomatoes, kidney beans, Halal beef broth, chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
4. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 20-30 minutes, allowing flavors to meld. Stir occasionally.
5. Serve hot, garnished with fresh coriander if desired.

snack.name: Apple slices with a small handful of walnuts
snack.description: Crisp apple slices paired with walnuts for a boost of healthy fats and fiber.
instructions: 1. Slice the apple.
2. Enjoy apple slices with walnuts.

ingredients: 1 large ripe banana, frozen and sliced
instructions: 1. Place frozen banana slices and almond milk (and cocoa powder if using) in a blender or food processor.
2. Blend until smooth and creamy, scraping down the sides as needed. It should resemble soft-serve ice cream.
3. Serve immediately.
1. Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F).
2. In a bowl, toss frozen edamame with olive oil, chili powder, garlic powder, and salt.
3. Spread in a single layer on a baking tray lined with parchment paper.
4. Roast for 15-20 minutes, stirring halfway, until slightly crispy and heated through.

proteins: 1 Halal whey protein powder (vanilla or unflavored)
dairy_alternatives: 1 small can light coconut milk (Halal certified)

breakfast.name: Scrambled Eggs with Spinach and Wholegrain Toast
breakfast.description: Fluffy scrambled eggs with wilted spinach and a slice of wholegrain toast.
ingredients: pinch of salt and black pepper
breakfast.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat. 2. Add spinach and cook until wilted. 3. Whisk eggs with salt and pepper, then pour into the pan. 4. Scramble until cooked through. 5. Serve with wholegrain toast.

lunch.name: Lentil Soup with Whole Wheat Bread
lunch.description: Hearty and warming lentil soup packed with vegetables, served with whole wheat bread.
lunch.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a pot. Sauté onion, carrots, celery, and garlic until softened. 2. Add lentils, vegetable broth, cumin, turmeric, salt, and pepper. 3. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes. 4. Serve hot with a slice of whole wheat bread.

dinner.name: Baked Halal Chicken with Steamed Broccoli and Quinoa
dinner.description: Tender baked halal chicken breast with nutrient-dense quinoa and fresh steamed broccoli.
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (390°F). 2. Season chicken breast with paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. 3. Place chicken on a baking tray and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until cooked through. 4. Steam broccoli until tender-crisp. 5. Serve chicken with quinoa and broccoli.

snack.name: Apple Slices with Almond Butter
snack.description: Crisp apple slices paired with creamy almond butter for a satisfying snack.
snack.instructions: Wash and slice the apple. Serve with almond butter for dipping.

breakfast.name: Oatmeal with Berries and Walnuts
breakfast.description: Warm oatmeal topped with a mix of fresh berries and crunchy walnuts.
ingredients: 200ml water or unsweetened almond milk (halal certified)
breakfast.instructions: 1. Combine oats and water/almond milk in a small saucepan. 2. Bring to a simmer over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until oats are cooked and liquid is absorbed (about 5-7 minutes). 3. Pour into a bowl, top with mixed berries and chopped walnuts.

lunch.name: Tuna Salad Lettuce Wraps
lunch.description: Light and refreshing tuna salad served in crisp lettuce cups.
lunch.instructions: 1. In a small bowl, flake the drained tuna. 2. Add mayonnaise, diced celery, red onion, salt, and pepper. Mix well. 3. Spoon the tuna salad into the lettuce leaves and serve.

dinner.name: Halal Beef Stir-fry with Brown Rice
dinner.description: Quick and flavorful stir-fry with tender halal beef, mixed vegetables, and brown rice.
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a large pan or wok over high heat. 2. Add beef strips and stir-fry until browned. Remove beef from the pan and set aside. 3. Add broccoli, bell pepper, and snap peas to the pan. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until tender-crisp. 4. Return beef to the pan. Add soy sauce, rice vinegar, garlic, and ginger. Toss to combine. 5. Serve the stir-fry over brown rice and sprinkle with sesame seeds.

snack.name: Greek Yogurt with Honey
snack.description: Creamy plain Greek yogurt drizzled with a touch of honey.
snack.instructions: Spoon Greek yogurt into a bowl and drizzle with honey.

breakfast.name: Smoothie Bowl with Banana and Chia Seeds
breakfast.description: Thick and creamy smoothie bowl topped with banana slices and chia seeds.
breakfast.instructions: 1. Combine frozen banana, almond milk, and spinach in a blender. Blend until smooth and thick, adding more almond milk if needed to reach desired consistency. 2. Pour into a bowl. Top with chia seeds and mixed berries.

lunch.name: Hummus and Vegetable Wrap
lunch.description: Whole wheat wrap filled with creamy hummus and fresh crunchy vegetables.
lunch.instructions: 1. Lay the tortilla flat. Spread hummus evenly over the tortilla. 2. Arrange cucumber, bell pepper, shredded carrots, and spinach over the hummus. 3. Roll up tightly and slice in half if desired.

dinner.name: Halal Ground Chicken and Vegetable Skillet
dinner.description: One-pan skillet meal with lean halal ground chicken and a medley of colorful vegetables.
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. 2. Add ground chicken and cook, breaking it apart with a spoon, until browned. Drain any excess fat. 3. Add onion and garlic to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes until softened. 4. Stir in zucchini, cherry tomatoes, oregano, salt, and pepper. Cook for another 5-7 minutes, until vegetables are tender-crisp. 5. Serve hot.

snack.name: Rice Cakes with Avocado
snack.description: Crispy rice cakes topped with creamy avocado and a sprinkle of chili flakes.
snack.instructions: Top each rice cake with half of the mashed avocado. Sprinkle with chili flakes if desired.

breakfast.name: Greek Yogurt with Granola and Peach
breakfast.description: Creamy Greek yogurt layered with crunchy granola and fresh peach slices.
breakfast.instructions: Layer Greek yogurt, granola, and sliced peach in a bowl or glass.

lunch.name: Chicken and Vegetable Skewers with Quinoa
lunch.description: Grilled halal chicken and colorful vegetable skewers, served with fluffy quinoa.
lunch.instructions: 1. If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes. 2. Thread chicken, bell pepper, zucchini, and red onion onto skewers. 3. Drizzle with olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, salt, and pepper. 4. Grill skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender. 5. Serve with cooked quinoa.

dinner.name: Salmon with Roasted Asparagus and Sweet Potato
dinner.description: Flaky baked salmon fillet with nutritious roasted asparagus and sweet potato wedges.
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (390°F). 2. Toss sweet potato wedges with half of the olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 15 minutes. 3. Toss asparagus spears with remaining olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. 4. Add salmon fillet and asparagus to the baking sheet with the sweet potatoes. Bake for another 12-15 minutes, or until salmon is cooked through and flakes easily, and vegetables are tender.

snack.name: Hard-boiled Egg and Cherry Tomatoes
snack.description: Simple and protein-rich snack with a hard-boiled egg and juicy cherry tomatoes.
snack.instructions: Peel the hard-boiled egg. Serve with cherry tomatoes.

breakfast.name: Cottage Cheese with Pineapple and Toasted Almonds
breakfast.description: Creamy cottage cheese topped with sweet pineapple chunks and crunchy toasted almonds.
ingredients: 80g fresh or canned pineapple chunks (in juice, drained)
breakfast.instructions: Combine cottage cheese, pineapple, and almonds in a bowl.

lunch.name: Chickpea Salad Sandwich (on Wholegrain)
lunch.description: A flavorful vegetarian sandwich made with mashed chickpeas, veggies, and wholegrain bread.
lunch.instructions: 1. In a bowl, mash chickpeas with a fork. 2. Stir in mayonnaise, celery, red onion, parsley, salt, and pepper. 3. Spread the chickpea salad onto one slice of wholegrain bread, top with lettuce, and then the second slice of bread.

dinner.name: Halal Turkey Meatloaf Muffins with Green Beans
dinner.description: Portion-controlled turkey meatloaf muffins served with crisp green beans.
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Lightly grease a muffin tin. 2. In a bowl, combine ground turkey, breadcrumbs, egg, onion, Worcestershire sauce, mixed herbs, salt, and pepper. Mix until just combined. 3. Divide the mixture into 2 muffin cups, forming small loaves. 4. Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until cooked through. 5. While meatloaf bakes, heat olive oil in a pan. Add green beans and sauté until tender-crisp. 6. Serve meatloaf muffins with sautéed green beans.

snack.name: Orange and Handful of Almonds
snack.description: Refreshing orange paired with a small portion of healthy almonds.
snack.instructions: Peel the orange. Serve with almonds.

breakfast.name: Breakfast Quesadilla with Black Beans and Cheese
breakfast.description: A quick and filling quesadilla with black beans, cheese, and a hint of spice.
ingredients: pinch of chili powder and cumin
breakfast.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat. 2. Pour whisked egg into the pan and cook briefly to form a thin omelet. 3. Place tortilla in the pan. Layer half of the cheese, then the egg omelet, black beans, chili powder, cumin, and remaining cheese. 4. Fold the tortilla in half and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, until cheese is melted and tortilla is golden brown.

lunch.name: Chicken and Vegetable Noodle Soup
lunch.description: Light and comforting soup with tender halal chicken, noodles, and mixed vegetables.
ingredients: 80g halal chicken breast, cooked and shredded
lunch.instructions: 1. Bring chicken broth to a simmer in a pot. 2. Add carrots and celery, cook for 5 minutes. 3. Add noodles and cook according to package directions. 4. Stir in shredded chicken and spinach. Cook until spinach is wilted and soup is heated through. Season with salt and pepper.

dinner.name: Halal Lamb Tagine with Couscous
dinner.description: Fragrant and tender halal lamb tagine with spices and vegetables, served with fluffy couscous.
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a pot or tagine over medium heat. Add lamb and brown on all sides. Remove lamb and set aside. 2. Add onion to the pot and cook until softened. Stir in ginger, cumin, and cinnamon, and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. 3. Return lamb to the pot. Add diced tomatoes, bell pepper, and 100ml of vegetable broth. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat, cover, and cook for 45-60 minutes, or until lamb is tender. 4. Meanwhile, prepare couscous: bring remaining 100ml vegetable broth to a boil. Remove from heat, stir in couscous, cover, and let sit for 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork. 5. Serve lamb tagine over couscous, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.

snack.name: Handful of Dates and Walnuts
snack.description: Naturally sweet dates paired with nutritious walnuts for an energy boost.
snack.instructions: Enjoy dates and walnuts together.

breakfast.name: Ricotta and Berries on Whole Wheat Toast
breakfast.description: Creamy ricotta cheese spread on whole wheat toast, topped with fresh mixed berries.
breakfast.instructions: Toast the whole wheat bread. Spread ricotta cheese on top, then arrange mixed berries.

lunch.name: Leftover Halal Lamb Tagine with Couscous
lunch.description: Enjoy the delicious leftovers from Saturday's dinner.
ingredients: Portion of Halal Lamb Tagine and Couscous (approx. 200g tagine, 80g couscous)
lunch.instructions: Reheat thoroughly in a microwave or on the stovetop until piping hot.

dinner.name: Baked Cod with Roasted Vegetables
dinner.description: Light and flavorful baked cod fillet served with a colorful medley of roasted vegetables.
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (390°F). 2. On a baking sheet, toss zucchini, bell pepper, and cherry tomatoes with half of the olive oil, salt, and pepper. 3. Place cod fillet on the same baking sheet. Drizzle cod with remaining olive oil, lemon juice, dill, salt, and pepper. 4. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until cod is opaque and flakes easily, and vegetables are tender.

snack.name: Pear with Small Handful of Pecans
snack.description: Sweet and juicy pear paired with crunchy pecans.
snack.instructions: Wash and slice the pear. Serve with pecans.

craving_alternatives.instructions: 1. In a food processor, blend dates until they form a sticky paste. 2. Add cocoa powder, rolled oats, and salt. Process until well combined. If mixture is too dry, add water 5ml at a time. 3. Roll the mixture into small, bite-sized balls. 4. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to firm up before eating.

ingredients: 1 can (400g) chickpeas, rinsed and thoroughly drained
craving_alternatives.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). 2. Pat chickpeas very dry with a paper towel. This is key for crispiness. 3. In a bowl, toss chickpeas with olive oil, smoked paprika, cumin, cayenne pepper (if using), and salt. 4. Spread chickpeas in a single layer on a baking sheet. 5. Roast for 25-35 minutes, stirring halfway through, until golden brown and crispy.

breakfast.name: Oatmeal with Sliced Banana and Cinnamon
breakfast.description: Classic comforting oatmeal topped with sweet banana slices and a sprinkle of warm cinnamon.
ingredients: 200ml water or unsweetened almond milk (halal certified)
breakfast.instructions: 1. Combine oats and water/almond milk in a small saucepan. 2. Bring to a simmer over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until oats are cooked and liquid is absorbed (about 5-7 minutes). 3. Pour into a bowl, top with sliced banana and a sprinkle of cinnamon.

lunch.name: Halal Turkey and Hummus Lettuce Wraps
lunch.description: Lean halal turkey slices with creamy hummus and crisp vegetables wrapped in lettuce.
lunch.instructions: 1. Spread hummus evenly onto each lettuce leaf. 2. Layer with turkey slices, cucumber, and shredded carrots. 3. Roll up each lettuce leaf tightly. Sprinkle with black pepper.

dinner.name: Halal Chicken Curry with Brown Rice
dinner.description: Mild and flavorful halal chicken curry with vegetables, served over steamed brown rice.
ingredients: 10g mild curry paste (halal certified, no alcohol)
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add chicken and cook until lightly browned. Remove and set aside. 2. Add onion to the pan and cook until softened. Stir in curry paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. 3. Return chicken to the pan. Add bell pepper, zucchini, and coconut milk. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat and cook for 10-15 minutes, until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender. 4. Serve curry over brown rice, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.

snack.name: Clementine and 10 Almonds
snack.description: Sweet clementine and a small handful of almonds for a quick and easy snack.
snack.instructions: Peel the clementine and enjoy with almonds.

breakfast.name: Avocado Toast with Egg
breakfast.description: Creamy avocado spread on wholegrain toast, topped with a fried or poached egg.
breakfast.instructions: 1. Toast the wholegrain bread. 2. While toast is cooking, fry or poach the egg to your liking. 3. Spread mashed avocado over the toast. Top with the cooked egg. 4. Season with salt, pepper, and chili flakes if desired.

lunch.name: Quinoa Salad with Roasted Vegetables
lunch.description: Nutrient-packed quinoa salad with roasted bell peppers, zucchini, and a lemon dressing.
lunch.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (390°F). Toss bell pepper and zucchini with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast for 15-20 minutes until tender. 2. In a bowl, combine cooked quinoa, roasted vegetables, lemon juice, and chopped parsley. Mix well. 3. Serve chilled or at room temperature.

dinner.name: Halal Ground Lamb Skillet with Eggplant and Tomatoes
dinner.description: Hearty skillet meal featuring flavorful halal ground lamb with tender eggplant and juicy tomatoes.
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. 2. Add ground lamb and cook, breaking it apart, until browned. Drain any excess fat. 3. Add onion and garlic to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes until softened. 4. Stir in eggplant, diced tomatoes, dried mint, salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until eggplant is tender.

snack.name: Greek Yogurt with Berries
snack.description: Creamy plain Greek yogurt topped with a generous serving of fresh berries.
snack.instructions: Spoon Greek yogurt into a bowl and top with mixed berries.

breakfast.name: Spinach and Mushroom Omelette
breakfast.description: A fluffy omelette packed with sautéed spinach and mushrooms.
ingredients: pinch of salt and black pepper
breakfast.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat. 2. Add mushrooms and cook until softened. Add spinach and cook until wilted. Remove vegetables from pan and set aside. 3. Whisk eggs with salt and pepper. Pour into the same pan. 4. Cook for 2-3 minutes until edges set. Add spinach and mushrooms to one half of the omelette. 5. Fold the other half over and cook for another minute until heated through.

lunch.name: Mediterranean Chickpea Salad
lunch.description: A fresh and vibrant salad with chickpeas, cucumber, tomatoes, and a light lemon-herb dressing.
lunch.instructions: 1. In a large bowl, combine chickpeas, cucumber, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and parsley. 2. In a small bowl, whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper. 3. Pour dressing over the salad and toss to combine. Serve chilled.

dinner.name: Halal Prawn and Vegetable Skewers with Brown Rice
dinner.description: Grilled or baked prawn and vegetable skewers, served with steamed brown rice.
dinner.instructions: 1. If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes. 2. In a bowl, toss prawns, bell pepper, red onion, and zucchini with olive oil, paprika, minced garlic, salt, and pepper. 3. Thread the prawns and vegetables onto skewers. 4. Grill or bake (at 200°C/390°F) for 8-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until prawns are pink and cooked through and vegetables are tender. 5. Serve skewers with brown rice.

snack.name: Rice Cakes with Sliced Banana
snack.description: Light and crispy rice cakes topped with banana slices.
snack.instructions: Top each rice cake with banana slices.

breakfast.name: Whole Wheat Pancakes with Berries
breakfast.description: Healthy whole wheat pancakes topped with a colorful mix of fresh berries.
ingredients: 50g whole wheat pancake mix (halal certified, follow package for water/milk)
breakfast.instructions: 1. Prepare pancake mix according to package directions using water or unsweetened almond milk. 2. Cook pancakes on a non-stick griddle or pan until golden brown on both sides. 3. Serve with mixed berries and a drizzle of maple syrup if desired.

lunch.name: Lentil and Vegetable Soup with a Small Apple
lunch.description: Hearty and nutritious lentil soup with a side of crisp apple.
lunch.instructions: Reheat lentil soup until hot. Enjoy with a fresh apple.

dinner.name: Baked Halal Haddock with Roasted Root Vegetables
dinner.description: Flaky baked haddock fillet served with a medley of flavorful roasted root vegetables.
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (390°F). 2. On a baking sheet, toss carrots and parsnips with half of the olive oil, salt, pepper, and dried thyme. Roast for 20 minutes. 3. Place haddock fillet on the same baking sheet. Drizzle haddock with remaining olive oil, salt, and pepper. 4. Bake for another 12-15 minutes, or until fish is opaque and flakes easily and vegetables are tender. 5. Serve with lemon wedges.

snack.name: Cottage Cheese with Cucumber Sticks
snack.description: Creamy cottage cheese paired with refreshing cucumber sticks.
snack.instructions: Serve cottage cheese with cucumber sticks. Season with black pepper.

breakfast.name: Hard-boiled Eggs with Wholegrain Toast
breakfast.description: Two protein-rich hard-boiled eggs served with a slice of wholegrain toast.
breakfast.instructions: Peel the hard-boiled eggs. Serve with wholegrain toast, seasoned with salt and pepper.

lunch.name: Leftover Halal Chicken Curry with Brown Rice
lunch.description: Enjoy the delicious leftovers from Monday's dinner.
ingredients: Portion of Halal Chicken Curry and Brown Rice (approx. 200g curry, 100g rice)
lunch.instructions: Reheat thoroughly in a microwave or on the stovetop until piping hot.

dinner.name: Halal Chicken and Vegetable Stir-fry
dinner.description: Quick and healthy stir-fry with tender halal chicken and a colorful mix of vegetables.
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a large pan or wok over high heat. Add chicken slices and stir-fry until browned and cooked through. Remove chicken and set aside. 2. Add broccoli, bell pepper, and snap peas to the pan. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until tender-crisp. 3. Return chicken to the pan. Add soy sauce, rice vinegar, garlic, and ginger. Toss to combine. 4. Stir in cashews if using. Serve hot.

snack.name: Orange with Almond Butter
snack.description: Refreshing orange slices with a dollop of almond butter.
snack.instructions: Peel and segment the orange. Serve with almond butter.

breakfast.name: Fruit and Nut Oatmeal
breakfast.description: Warm oatmeal packed with dried fruits and a mix of nuts.
ingredients: 200ml water or unsweetened almond milk (halal certified)
20g mixed dried fruit (halal certified, e.g., raisins, apricots)
15g mixed nuts (halal certified, e.g., almonds, walnuts)
breakfast.instructions: 1. Combine oats and water/almond milk in a small saucepan. 2. Bring to a simmer over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until oats are cooked and liquid is absorbed (about 5-7 minutes). 3. Pour into a bowl, top with mixed dried fruit and mixed nuts.

lunch.name: Tuna and White Bean Salad
lunch.description: Protein-packed salad with canned tuna, white beans, and fresh vegetables.
ingredients: 100g canned cannellini beans, rinsed and drained
lunch.instructions: 1. In a bowl, combine flaked tuna, cannellini beans, red bell pepper, red onion, and parsley. 2. In a small bowl, whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper. 3. Pour dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine. Serve chilled.

dinner.name: Halal Beef and Vegetable Shepherd's Pie (Mini)
dinner.description: Individual portions of classic shepherd's pie with savory halal beef and a mashed sweet potato topping.
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (390°F). 2. Boil sweet potato cubes until very tender. Drain and mash, seasoning with salt and pepper. 3. While sweet potato boils, heat olive oil in a skillet. Add ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it apart. Drain any excess fat. 4. Stir in carrots, peas, beef broth, dried thyme, salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes until carrots are tender. 5. Divide the beef mixture into two oven-safe ramekins or a small oven-safe dish. Top with mashed sweet potato. 6. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is lightly browned and the filling is bubbling.

snack.name: Pear and Handful of Pecans
snack.description: Sweet and juicy pear paired with crunchy pecans.
snack.instructions: Wash and slice the pear. Serve with pecans.

breakfast.name: Greek Yogurt with Mixed Berries and Chia Seeds
breakfast.description: Protein-rich Greek yogurt topped with antioxidant-rich berries and healthy chia seeds.
breakfast.instructions: Combine Greek yogurt, mixed berries, and chia seeds in a bowl.

lunch.name: Leftover Halal Beef and Vegetable Shepherd's Pie
lunch.description: Enjoy the delicious leftovers from Saturday's dinner.
ingredients: Portion of Halal Beef and Vegetable Shepherd's Pie (approx. 250g)
lunch.instructions: Reheat thoroughly in a microwave or oven until piping hot.

dinner.name: Halal Lamb Kofta with Cucumber Mint Raita and Salad
dinner.description: Flavorful grilled halal lamb kofta served with a cooling cucumber mint raita and a fresh green salad.
dinner.instructions: 1. For kofta: In a bowl, combine ground lamb, grated red onion, chopped mint, cumin, coriander powder, salt, and pepper. Mix well. Form into 4-6 small sausage shapes or patties. 2. Grill or pan-fry kofta for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until cooked through and browned. 3. For raita: In a small bowl, combine Greek yogurt, grated cucumber, and chopped mint. Season with salt. 4. For salad: Toss mixed greens and cherry tomatoes with lemon juice and olive oil. 5. Serve kofta with raita and the green salad.

snack.name: Apple Slices with Cottage Cheese
snack.description: Crisp apple slices paired with creamy cottage cheese.
snack.instructions: Slice the apple and serve with cottage cheese.

craving_alternatives.instructions: 1. Line a small baking sheet with parchment paper. 2. Spread Greek yogurt evenly over the parchment paper to about 1/4 inch thickness. 3. Sprinkle with mixed berries and drizzle with honey/maple syrup. 4. Freeze for at least 2-3 hours, or until firm. Break into pieces to serve.

ingredients: 1 large bell pepper (any color), halved lengthwise, seeds removed
30g passata or low-sugar tomato sauce (halal certified)
craving_alternatives.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F). 2. Place bell pepper halves cut-side up on a baking sheet. 3. Spoon tomato sauce into each bell pepper half. Sprinkle with mozzarella cheese, turkey pepperoni (if using), and oregano. 4. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until cheese is melted and bubbly and peppers are tender-crisp.

grains: 1 small box whole wheat pancake mix (halal certified)
dairy_alternatives: 1 small bag low-fat shredded mozzarella (halal certified)
pantry: mild curry paste (halal certified, no alcohol)
passata or low-sugar tomato sauce (halal certified)

breakfast.name: Overnight Oats with Berries and Chia Seeds
breakfast.description: Creamy overnight oats packed with fiber and antioxidants.
ingredients: 180ml unsweetened almond milk (or other plant-based milk)
breakfast.instructions: 1. Combine oats, almond milk, and chia seeds in a jar or container. 2. Stir well and refrigerate overnight (or for at least 4 hours). 3. In the morning, top with mixed berries and a drizzle of honey if desired.

lunch.name: Quinoa Salad with Grilled Halal Chicken and Roasted Vegetables
lunch.description: A vibrant and filling salad with lean protein and wholesome grains.
ingredients: 100g Halal chicken breast, grilled and sliced
100g mixed roasted vegetables (e.g., zucchini, bell peppers, carrots)
Salt and black pepper to taste
lunch.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Toss chopped vegetables with a little olive oil, salt, and pepper, then roast for 15-20 minutes until tender. 2. Grill or pan-fry Halal chicken breast seasoned with salt and pepper until cooked through. Slice into strips. 3. In a bowl, combine cooked quinoa, roasted vegetables, spinach, and sliced chicken. 4. Drizzle with the remaining olive oil and lemon juice. Mix well.

dinner.name: Baked Halal Salmon with Asparagus and Brown Rice
dinner.description: Flaky salmon with tender asparagus and nutritious brown rice.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F). 2. Season salmon with salt, pepper, and dill. Place on a baking sheet. 3. Toss asparagus with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Add to the baking sheet with the salmon. 4. Bake for 12-15 minutes, or until salmon is cooked through and asparagus is tender-crisp. 5. Serve with cooked brown rice and a lemon wedge.

snack.name: Greek Yogurt with a Few Almonds
snack.description: A quick and protein-rich snack.
snack.instructions: Combine Greek yogurt with almonds and enjoy.

breakfast.name: Whole Wheat Toast with Avocado and Poached Egg
breakfast.description: A balanced breakfast with healthy fats and protein.
breakfast.instructions: 1. Toast the whole wheat bread. 2. While toast is cooking, poach the egg (bring water to a simmer, add a splash of vinegar, gently drop in egg, cook for 3-4 minutes). 3. Spread mashed avocado on toast. 4. Top with the poached egg, season with salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes if desired.

lunch.name: Lentil Soup with Whole Grain Bread
lunch.description: Hearty and warming lentil soup, rich in fiber.
ingredients: 250ml prepared lentil soup (homemade or low-sodium canned)
lunch.instructions: 1. Heat lentil soup until warm. 2. Drizzle bread with olive oil and toast lightly if desired. 3. Serve soup with bread, garnished with parsley.

dinner.name: Halal Ground Beef Stir-fry with Broccoli and Bell Peppers
dinner.description: Lean ground beef stir-fried with colorful vegetables.
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat sesame oil in a large pan or wok over medium-high heat. 2. Add ground beef and cook, breaking it up, until browned. Drain any excess fat. 3. Add ginger and garlic, stir-fry for 1 minute. 4. Add broccoli and bell peppers. Stir-fry for 5-7 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp. 5. Stir in soy sauce and mix well. Serve hot.

snack.name: Orange and Handful of Walnuts
snack.description: Refreshing fruit with healthy omega-3 fats.
snack.instructions: Peel the orange and enjoy with walnuts.

breakfast.name: Berry Smoothie with Protein Powder
breakfast.description: A quick and nutritious smoothie to kickstart your day.
ingredients: 1 scoop (25g) Halal-certified whey protein powder (vanilla or unflavored)
breakfast.instructions: 1. Combine all ingredients in a blender. 2. Blend until smooth. 3. Pour into a glass and enjoy immediately.

lunch.name: Chickpea and Vegetable Wrap
lunch.description: A flavorful vegetarian wrap, packed with fiber.
lunch.instructions: 1. In a bowl, mash chickpeas slightly with a fork. 2. Add cucumber, tomato, parsley, and lemon juice. Mix well. 3. Spread hummus evenly over the tortilla. 4. Layer with mixed greens, then the chickpea mixture. 5. Roll up the tortilla tightly and cut in half if desired.

dinner.name: Halal Chicken and Vegetable Skewers with Small Sweet Potato
dinner.description: Grilled chicken and vegetables on skewers, served with a baked sweet potato.
ingredients: Paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper to taste
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Pierce sweet potato several times with a fork and bake for 45-60 minutes, or until tender. 2. Thread chicken, zucchini, and red onion alternately onto skewers. 3. Drizzle skewers with olive oil and season with paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. 4. Grill skewers on a hot grill or grill pan for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender-crisp. 5. Serve skewers with the baked sweet potato.

snack.name: Apple Slices with Peanut Butter
snack.description: A classic, satisfying snack.
snack.instructions: Slice the apple and spread or dip in peanut butter.

breakfast.name: Scrambled Eggs with Spinach and Wholegrain Toast
breakfast.description: A protein-rich breakfast with a serving of greens.
ingredients: 5ml olive oil or a knob of Halal butter
breakfast.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil or butter in a non-stick pan over medium heat. 2. Add spinach and cook until wilted. 3. Whisk eggs with salt and pepper. 4. Pour eggs into the pan with spinach and scramble until cooked to your liking. 5. Serve with toasted whole wheat bread.

lunch.name: Brown Rice Bowl with Black Beans and Corn
lunch.description: A flavorful and fiber-packed vegetarian bowl.
lunch.instructions: 1. In a bowl, combine cooked brown rice, black beans, and corn. 2. Top with salsa and diced avocado. 3. Squeeze fresh lime juice over the top before serving.

dinner.name: Halal Turkey Meatballs with Zucchini Noodles and Tomato Sauce
dinner.description: Lean turkey meatballs with a lighter pasta alternative.
dinner.instructions: 1. Mix ground turkey with salt, pepper, and a pinch of garlic powder. Form into small meatballs (approx. 6-8 meatballs). 2. Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Cook meatballs until browned on all sides and cooked through (approx. 8-10 minutes). Remove from pan and set aside. 3. In the same pan, add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute. Add tomato sauce and bring to a simmer. 4. Add zucchini noodles to the sauce and cook for 2-3 minutes until tender-crisp. 5. Return meatballs to the pan, toss with sauce and zucchini noodles. Garnish with fresh basil.

snack.name: Rice Cakes with Cottage Cheese
snack.description: Crispy rice cakes topped with creamy cottage cheese.
snack.instructions: Spread cottage cheese on rice cakes and enjoy.

breakfast.name: Oat Porridge with Banana and Cinnamon
breakfast.description: Classic warm oat porridge with sweet banana and aromatic cinnamon.
breakfast.instructions: 1. In a small pot, combine oats and water/milk. 2. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until oats are creamy. 3. Pour into a bowl, top with banana slices, cinnamon, and a drizzle of honey if using.

lunch.name: Whole Wheat Pita with Hummus and Mixed Veggies
lunch.description: A light and fresh meal perfect for a midday boost.
lunch.instructions: 1. Warm pita bread slightly (optional). 2. Spread hummus inside the pita. 3. Stuff with sliced cucumber, shredded carrot, and bell pepper.

dinner.name: Halal Chicken and Vegetable Curry with Cauliflower Rice
dinner.description: A flavorful and aromatic curry, lightened with cauliflower rice.
ingredients: 80g mixed vegetables (e.g., peas, carrots, green beans)
10g mild curry paste (Halal certified, check ingredients for alcohol/gelatin)
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat coconut oil in a pan over medium heat. Add curry paste and grated ginger, cook for 1 minute until fragrant. 2. Add cubed chicken and cook until browned on all sides. 3. Pour in coconut milk and add mixed vegetables. Bring to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender. 4. While curry is cooking, prepare cauliflower rice according to package instructions or sauté fresh cauliflower rice in a separate pan for 3-5 minutes. 5. Serve the chicken curry over cauliflower rice. Garnish with fresh cilantro.

snack.name: Hard-boiled Egg and Cherry Tomatoes
snack.description: Simple, protein-packed snack with fresh vegetables.
snack.instructions: Peel the egg and enjoy with cherry tomatoes.

breakfast.name: Greek Yogurt Parfait with Granola and Fruit
breakfast.description: Layered Greek yogurt with crunchy granola and fresh fruit.
breakfast.instructions: 1. In a glass or bowl, layer Greek yogurt, granola, and fruit. 2. Repeat layers if desired. Serve immediately.

lunch.name: Tuna Salad (Water-packed) with Whole Wheat Crackers and Celery
lunch.description: Light and refreshing tuna salad with crunchy crackers and celery sticks.
ingredients: 1 can (80g drained) tuna in water (Halal certified)
lunch.instructions: 1. Drain tuna well. In a small bowl, flake tuna with a fork. 2. Stir in mayonnaise, diced celery, salt, and pepper. 3. Serve with whole wheat crackers and extra celery sticks for dipping.

dinner.name: Baked Halal Cod with Roasted Root Vegetables
dinner.description: Mild, flaky cod with sweet and earthy roasted root vegetables.
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). 2. Toss chopped carrots and parsnips with olive oil, rosemary, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet. Roast for 20 minutes. 3. Place cod fillet on the same baking sheet, season with salt and pepper, and top with lemon slices. 4. Continue baking for another 12-15 minutes, or until cod is cooked through and flakes easily, and vegetables are tender.

snack.name: Small Pear
snack.description: A simple and sweet fruit snack.
snack.instructions: Wash and enjoy the pear.

breakfast.name: Sweet Potato Hash with Fried Egg
breakfast.description: Savory sweet potato hash topped with a perfectly fried egg.
breakfast.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat. 2. Add diced sweet potato and bell pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes until sweet potatoes are tender and lightly browned. Season with salt, pepper, and paprika. 3. In a separate small pan, fry the egg to your liking (sunny-side up, over easy). 4. Serve the sweet potato hash topped with the fried egg.

lunch.name: Minestrone Soup with a Side of Fruit
lunch.description: A hearty and vegetable-rich soup, complemented by fresh fruit.
ingredients: 300ml prepared minestrone soup (low-sodium, check ingredients for Halal)
lunch.instructions: 1. Heat minestrone soup until warm. 2. Serve with a fresh apple on the side.

dinner.name: Halal Beef and Mushroom Skillet with Green Beans
dinner.description: Lean beef and mushrooms cooked in a savory skillet with tender green beans.
ingredients: 120g lean Halal beef sirloin or flank steak, sliced thin
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. 2. Add beef slices and cook quickly until browned. Remove beef from skillet and set aside. 3. Add mushrooms and green beans to the skillet. Stir-fry for 5-7 minutes until tender-crisp. 4. Return beef to the skillet. Add minced garlic, ginger powder, and soy sauce. Toss to combine. 5. Cook for another 1-2 minutes until heated through. Garnish with sesame seeds if desired.

snack.name: Rice Cakes with Sliced Banana
snack.description: Light and easy snack with natural sweetness.
snack.instructions: Top rice cakes with sliced banana.

craving_alternatives.instructions: 1. Blend dates in a food processor until a smooth paste forms. 2. Add cocoa powder, oats, and salt. Process until well combined and a sticky dough forms. Add water if mixture is too dry. 3. Roll into small balls (approx. 15-20g each). 4. Refrigerate for 30 minutes before eating.

1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). 2. Pat chickpeas very dry with a paper towel (this is key for crispiness!). 3. Toss with olive oil, paprika, cumin, and salt. 4. Spread in a single layer on a baking tray. 5. Bake for 25-30 minutes, shaking the tray halfway through, until crispy. Let cool slightly before eating.

breakfast.name: Mango and Coconut Chia Pudding
breakfast.description: A tropical-inspired chia pudding, great for meal prep.
breakfast.instructions: 1. In a jar or container, whisk together chia seeds and coconut milk until well combined. 2. Refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight, stirring once after 30 minutes to prevent clumping. 3. In the morning, top with mango chunks and desiccated coconut.

lunch.name: Chicken and Veggie Couscous Salad
lunch.description: Light and refreshing couscous salad with tender chicken and fresh vegetables.
ingredients: 100g Halal chicken breast, grilled and diced
15ml lemon vinaigrette (lemon juice, 10ml olive oil, salt, pepper)
lunch.instructions: 1. Prepare couscous according to package directions. Let cool. 2. Grill or pan-fry Halal chicken breast until cooked through, then dice. 3. In a large bowl, combine cooled couscous, diced chicken, cucumber, tomato, red onion, and chopped herbs. 4. Drizzle with lemon vinaigrette and toss gently to combine.

dinner.name: Baked Halal Haddock with Steamed Broccoli and Quinoa
dinner.description: Lean white fish served with nutrient-dense quinoa and green vegetables.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F). Place haddock on a baking sheet, drizzle with olive oil, season with salt and pepper, and top with a lemon wedge. 2. Bake for 12-15 minutes, or until haddock is cooked through and flakes easily. 3. Steam broccoli until tender-crisp. 4. Serve baked haddock with steamed broccoli and cooked quinoa.

snack.name: Small handful of pistachios
snack.description: Crunchy and satisfying nuts.
snack.instructions: Enjoy pistachios as a healthy snack.

breakfast.name: Peanut Butter Banana Toast
breakfast.description: A classic combination for a quick, energy-boosting breakfast.
breakfast.instructions: 1. Toast the whole wheat bread. 2. Spread peanut butter evenly on each slice. 3. Top with sliced banana and a sprinkle of chia seeds if desired.

lunch.name: Leftover Haddock and Quinoa Bowl
lunch.description: Enjoy the delicious leftovers from yesterday's dinner.
lunch.instructions: 1. Flake leftover haddock into pieces. 2. In a bowl, combine quinoa, mixed greens, cherry tomatoes, and flaked haddock. 3. Drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice. Toss gently.

dinner.name: Halal Lamb Tagine-style Stew with Chickpeas
dinner.description: A fragrant and hearty lamb stew with warming spices and chickpeas.
ingredients: 150ml low-sodium chicken or vegetable broth (Halal certified)
10g tagine spice blend (check for Halal ingredients, no alcohol)
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown cubed lamb on all sides. Remove lamb and set aside. 2. Add onion, carrot, and zucchini to the pot. Sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened. 3. Return lamb to the pot. Stir in tagine spice blend and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. 4. Pour in broth, bring to a simmer, then cover and reduce heat to low. Cook for 45-60 minutes, or until lamb is tender. 5. Stir in chickpeas and cook for another 5 minutes to heat through. 6. Serve garnished with fresh cilantro.

snack.name: Rice Cakes with Sliced Cucumber
snack.description: A light and refreshing snack.
snack.instructions: Top rice cakes with sliced cucumber.

breakfast.name: Savory Oats with Egg and Hot Sauce
breakfast.description: A unique and filling twist on traditional oatmeal.
breakfast.instructions: 1. Cook oats with water or broth according to package directions. 2. While oats are cooking, fry or poach the egg to your preference. 3. Stir soy sauce into cooked oats. 4. Top oats with the cooked egg, chopped green onions, and hot sauce if desired.

lunch.name: Spicy Halal Chicken and Avocado Salad
lunch.description: A protein-packed salad with a kick, served on a bed of greens.
ingredients: 120g Halal chicken breast, cooked and shredded or diced
Pinch of cayenne pepper or chilli flakes
lunch.instructions: 1. In a bowl, combine shredded chicken, diced avocado, red onion, cilantro, light mayonnaise, cayenne pepper, salt, and pepper. Mix well. 2. Serve the chicken salad over a bed of mixed greens.

dinner.name: Halal Prawn Stir-fry with Snow Peas and Brown Rice
dinner.description: Quick and healthy prawn stir-fry with crisp snow peas.
ingredients: 120g raw Halal prawns, peeled and deveined
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat sesame oil in a large pan or wok over medium-high heat. 2. Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant. 3. Add prawns and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until pink and cooked through. 4. Add snow peas and stir-fry for another 2-3 minutes until tender-crisp. 5. Stir in soy sauce and toss to combine. 6. Serve the prawn stir-fry immediately with cooked brown rice.

snack.name: Small pot of Greek Yogurt with a few berries
snack.description: Creamy yogurt with a touch of sweetness.
snack.instructions: Combine Greek yogurt with berries and enjoy.

breakfast.name: Breakfast Burrito Bowl
breakfast.description: A deconstructed breakfast burrito with high carbs and protein.
breakfast.instructions: 1. In a pan, scramble eggs with salt and pepper. 2. In another pan, quickly sauté spinach until wilted. 3. In a bowl, combine cooked brown rice, black beans, scrambled eggs, sautéed spinach, and salsa. Sprinkle with cumin.

lunch.name: Sweet Potato and Black Bean Chili
lunch.description: A hearty and flavorful vegetarian chili, perfect for meal prep.
ingredients: 300ml prepared sweet potato and black bean chili (homemade or low-sodium canned, Halal certified)
lunch.instructions: 1. Heat chili until warm. 2. Serve chili with a whole wheat roll on the side. Garnish with cilantro if desired.

dinner.name: Halal Chicken Breast with Roasted Brussels Sprouts and Carrot Mash
dinner.description: Lean chicken breast with nutrient-rich roasted Brussels sprouts and creamy carrot mash.
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). 2. Toss Brussels sprouts with 5ml olive oil, salt, pepper, and thyme. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes until tender and slightly caramelized. 3. While Brussels sprouts are roasting, season chicken breast with salt and pepper. Pan-fry or grill chicken for 6-8 minutes per side, or until cooked through. 4. Steam or boil chopped carrots until very tender. Mash them (can add a tiny splash of water or milk for creaminess, if desired). Season with salt and pepper. 5. Serve chicken breast with roasted Brussels sprouts and carrot mash.

snack.name: Handful of Dates
snack.description: Naturally sweet and energizing.
snack.instructions: Enjoy dates as a quick energy boost.

breakfast.name: Berry and Nut Butter Oatmeal
breakfast.description: Warm oatmeal with sweet berries and creamy nut butter.
breakfast.instructions: 1. In a small pot, combine oats and water/milk. 2. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally. 3. Pour into a bowl, stir in almond butter, and top with mixed berries. Drizzle with honey if desired.

lunch.name: Mediterranean Lentil Salad
lunch.description: A light and zesty salad with lentils, vegetables, and a lemon-herb dressing.
ingredients: 15ml lemon vinaigrette (lemon juice, 10ml olive oil, salt, pepper)
lunch.instructions: 1. In a bowl, combine cooked lentils, cucumber, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and parsley. 2. Drizzle with lemon vinaigrette and toss gently to combine.

dinner.name: Halal Lean Ground Beef and Vegetable Chili (no beans)
dinner.description: A warm and comforting chili, rich in vegetables and lean protein.
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat. Add onion and bell pepper, sauté for 5 minutes until softened. 2. Add ground beef and cook, breaking it up, until browned. Drain any excess fat. 3. Stir in chili powder, cumin, cayenne pepper (if using), salt, and pepper. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant. 4. Add diced tomatoes (with their juice) and bring to a simmer. Reduce heat and cook for 15-20 minutes, allowing flavors to meld. Serve hot.

snack.name: Edamame in Pods
snack.description: Fun and fibrous snack.
snack.instructions: Steam edamame according to package directions. Sprinkle with a little salt and enjoy.

breakfast.name: Ricotta and Berries with a Sprinkle of Granola
breakfast.description: Creamy ricotta cheese topped with fresh berries and a touch of crunch.
breakfast.instructions: 1. Place ricotta cheese in a bowl. 2. Top with mixed berries and sprinkle with granola. Drizzle with honey if desired.

lunch.name: Tuna and White Bean Salad
lunch.description: A Mediterranean-inspired salad with tuna and cannellini beans.
ingredients: 1 can (80g drained) tuna in water (Halal certified)
80g canned cannellini beans, rinsed and drained
15ml lemon vinaigrette (lemon juice, 10ml olive oil, salt, pepper)
lunch.instructions: 1. Drain tuna well and flake into a bowl. 2. Add cannellini beans, diced red bell pepper, red onion, and parsley. 3. Drizzle with lemon vinaigrette and toss gently to combine.

dinner.name: Halal Chicken and Bell Pepper Fajita Bowl
dinner.description: Flavorful fajita-spiced chicken and bell peppers, served as a bowl.
ingredients: 120g Halal chicken breast, sliced into strips
50g plain Greek yogurt (0% fat, as a sour cream alternative)
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. 2. Add chicken strips and cook until lightly browned. 3. Add sliced bell pepper and onion, stir in fajita seasoning. Cook for 7-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender-crisp. 4. Serve the fajita mixture in a bowl, topped with salsa and a dollop of Greek yogurt. Garnish with fresh cilantro.

snack.name: Hard-boiled Egg
snack.description: A simple and quick protein boost.
snack.instructions: Peel and enjoy a hard-boiled egg.

breakfast.name: Banana Protein Pancakes
breakfast.description: Fluffy pancakes made healthier with banana and protein.
ingredients: 1 scoop (25g) Halal-certified whey protein powder (vanilla or unflavored)
breakfast.instructions: 1. In a bowl, mash the banana. 2. Add egg, protein powder, whole wheat flour, and baking powder. Mix until just combined (do not overmix). 3. Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat with olive oil or cooking spray. 4. Pour about 1/4 cup of batter per pancake. Cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until golden brown and cooked through. 5. Serve with a drizzle of maple syrup if desired.

lunch.name: Leftover Halal Beef and Vegetable Chili
lunch.description: Enjoy the flavorful leftover chili from Friday's dinner.
ingredients: 300g Halal Lean Ground Beef and Vegetable Chili (from Friday)
lunch.instructions: 1. Reheat leftover chili until warm. 2. Serve with a whole wheat roll on the side.

dinner.name: Halal Lamb Chops with Roasted Cauliflower and Green Salad
dinner.description: Tender lamb chops with roasted cauliflower and a fresh green salad.
ingredients: Garlic powder, dried rosemary, salt, and black pepper to taste
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Toss cauliflower florets with olive oil, salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes until tender and slightly browned. 2. Season lamb chops with salt, pepper, and dried rosemary. 3. Heat a grill pan or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat. Sear lamb chops for 3-5 minutes per side for medium-rare, or longer for desired doneness. 4. In a small bowl, combine mixed greens and cherry tomatoes. Dress with lemon juice and a pinch of salt. 5. Serve lamb chops with roasted cauliflower and the green salad.

snack.name: Small Apple with a few Walnuts
snack.description: A classic pairing of fruit and healthy fats.
snack.instructions: Wash the apple and enjoy with walnuts.

craving_alternatives.instructions: 1. Place frozen banana slices and almond milk in a food processor or high-speed blender. 2. Blend until smooth and creamy, like soft-serve ice cream. Add cocoa powder if making chocolate flavor. 3. Serve immediately.
1. Wash and slice the apple. 2. Sprinkle with cinnamon.

proteins: 2 cans (80g drained each) tuna in water (Halal certified)
Halal-certified whey protein powder (vanilla or unflavored)
pantry: Low-sodium chicken or vegetable broth (Halal certified)

breakfast.name: Overnight Oats with Berries and Almonds
breakfast.description: Chilled oats soaked in almond milk, topped with fresh berries and crunchy almonds.
breakfast.instructions: 1. Combine oats, almond milk, chia seeds, and maple syrup in a jar. Stir well. 2. Cover and refrigerate overnight. 3. In the morning, top with mixed berries and sliced almonds before serving.

lunch.name: Grilled Halal Chicken Salad with Tahini Dressing
lunch.description: Lean grilled chicken breast over a bed of fresh mixed greens, cucumber, tomatoes, and a creamy tahini dressing.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
lunch.instructions: 1. Season chicken breast with salt and pepper, grill until cooked through (approx. 6-8 minutes per side). Let rest, then slice. 2. In a small bowl, whisk together tahini, water, lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper for the dressing. 3. Arrange mixed greens, cucumber, tomatoes, and red onion in a bowl. Top with sliced chicken. 4. Drizzle with tahini dressing and serve.

dinner.name: Halal Beef and Broccoli Stir-fry with Brown Rice
dinner.description: Tender strips of Halal beef and crisp broccoli florets tossed in a savory ginger-garlic sauce, served with wholesome brown rice.
ingredients: 150g lean Halal beef sirloin, thinly sliced
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a large pan or wok over medium-high heat. Add beef and stir-fry until browned. Remove beef and set aside. 2. Add broccoli to the pan and stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until tender-crisp. 3. In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, sesame oil, grated ginger, minced garlic, and water. Add cornstarch slurry. 4. Return beef to the pan. Pour the sauce over beef and broccoli. Cook, stirring, until the sauce thickens. 5. Serve immediately over cooked brown rice.

snack.name: Apple Slices with Almond Butter
snack.description: Crisp apple slices paired with natural almond butter for a satisfying snack.
snack.instructions: Slice the apple and serve with almond butter for dipping.

breakfast.name: Scrambled Eggs with Spinach and Wholemeal Toast
breakfast.description: Fluffy scrambled eggs cooked with fresh spinach, served with a slice of wholemeal toast.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
breakfast.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat. 2. Whisk eggs with salt and pepper. Pour into the pan. 3. As eggs begin to set, add spinach and gently stir until spinach wilts and eggs are cooked to your liking. 4. Toast the wholemeal bread. 5. Serve scrambled eggs alongside the toast.

lunch.name: Mediterranean Lentil Salad
lunch.description: A refreshing and protein-rich salad with cooked lentils, fresh vegetables, and a light lemon-herb dressing.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
lunch.instructions: 1. In a large bowl, combine cooked lentils, cucumber, bell pepper, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and fresh parsley. 2. In a small bowl, whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper. 3. Pour the dressing over the lentil mixture and toss gently to combine. 4. Serve chilled or at room temperature.

dinner.name: Baked Halal Salmon with Asparagus and Quinoa
dinner.description: Flavorful baked salmon fillet paired with tender asparagus and fluffy quinoa for a balanced and healthy dinner.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). 2. Place salmon fillet on a baking sheet. Drizzle with 5ml olive oil, lemon juice, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. 3. On the same baking sheet, toss asparagus with the remaining 10ml olive oil, salt, and pepper. 4. Bake for 12-18 minutes, or until salmon is cooked through and asparagus is tender-crisp. 5. Serve salmon and asparagus alongside cooked quinoa.

snack.name: Rice Cakes with Avocado
snack.description: Light rice cakes topped with creamy avocado slices and a sprinkle of 'everything but the bagel' seasoning.
ingredients: 2g 'everything but the bagel' seasoning (ensure Halal certified, no sesame oil if alcohol-containing)
snack.instructions: Top rice cakes with sliced avocado and sprinkle with seasoning.

breakfast.name: Fruit Smoothie with Vegan Protein
breakfast.description: A refreshing blend of mixed berries, banana, and plant-based protein powder for a quick and nutritious start.
breakfast.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender and blend until smooth. Serve immediately.

lunch.name: Chickpea and Vegetable Wrap
lunch.description: A wholesome wrap filled with a flavorful chickpea salad and crisp mixed vegetables.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
lunch.instructions: 1. In a medium bowl, lightly mash chickpeas with a fork. 2. Stir in vegan mayonnaise, celery, red onion, dill, salt, and pepper until well combined. 3. Lay out the tortilla. Spread the chickpea mixture evenly, leaving a border. 4. Top with mixed greens and shredded carrots. 5. Roll up the tortilla tightly and slice in half if desired.

dinner.name: Halal Turkey Meatballs with Zucchini Noodles and Marinara
dinner.description: Lean Halal turkey meatballs simmered in a rich marinara sauce, served over light zucchini noodles.
ingredients: 150ml marinara sauce (ensure Halal certified, no alcohol)
Salt and black pepper to taste
dinner.instructions: 1. In a bowl, combine ground turkey, egg, breadcrumbs, parsley, garlic, salt, and pepper. Mix well and form into small meatballs (approx. 10-12 meatballs). 2. Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add meatballs and brown on all sides. 3. Pour marinara sauce over the meatballs, bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes, or until meatballs are cooked through. 4. While meatballs are simmering, gently sauté zucchini noodles in a separate pan for 2-3 minutes until tender-crisp. 5. Serve meatballs and sauce over zucchini noodles.

snack.name: Handful of Walnuts and Dates
snack.description: A natural energy boost from sweet dates and omega-3 rich walnuts.
snack.instructions: Enjoy walnuts and dates as a quick snack.

breakfast.name: Warm Quinoa Porridge with Cinnamon and Banana
breakfast.description: A warm and comforting porridge made with quinoa, flavored with cinnamon and topped with banana slices.
breakfast.instructions: 1. Rinse quinoa thoroughly. 2. In a small pot, combine quinoa, almond milk, and cinnamon. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes or until liquid is absorbed and quinoa is fluffy. 3. Stir in maple syrup. 4. Serve in a bowl, topped with banana slices.

lunch.name: Chicken and Vegetable Skewers with Hummus
lunch.description: Grilled Halal chicken and colorful vegetables on skewers, served with creamy hummus.
ingredients: 150g Halal chicken breast, cut into 1-inch cubes
50g bell pepper (any color), cut into 1-inch pieces
Salt, black pepper, paprika to taste
lunch.instructions: 1. If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes. 2. Toss chicken and vegetables with olive oil, salt, pepper, and paprika. 3. Thread chicken and vegetables alternately onto skewers. 4. Grill skewers over medium-high heat for 10-15 minutes, turning occasionally, until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender-crisp. 5. Serve skewers with hummus on the side.

dinner.name: Halal Lamb Tagine with Couscous
dinner.description: Slow-cooked Halal lamb with aromatic spices and vegetables, served with fluffy couscous.
ingredients: 150ml hot water or Halal vegetable broth
5g Halal certified tagine spice mix (cumin, coriander, turmeric, ginger, cinnamon, paprika)
Salt and black pepper to taste
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown lamb on all sides. Remove lamb and set aside. 2. Add onion and cook until softened (3-5 minutes). Add garlic and tagine spices, cook for 1 minute until fragrant. 3. Return lamb to the pot. Add carrots, zucchini, and diced tomatoes. Pour in enough water or broth to just cover the lamb. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 1.5-2 hours, or until lamb is very tender. 4. While lamb is cooking, prepare couscous: Place couscous in a heatproof bowl. Pour hot water or broth over it (1:1 ratio). Cover and let stand for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork. 5. Season tagine with salt and pepper to taste. Serve the lamb tagine over couscous, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.

snack.name: Orange
snack.description: A refreshing and juicy orange, packed with Vitamin C.
snack.instructions: Peel and enjoy.

breakfast.name: Avocado Toast with Hard-boiled Egg
breakfast.description: Creamy avocado spread on wholemeal toast, topped with a sliced hard-boiled egg.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
breakfast.instructions: 1. Toast the wholemeal bread. 2. Mash avocado with salt and pepper. Spread evenly on the toast. 3. Top with sliced hard-boiled egg and a pinch of red pepper flakes if desired.

lunch.name: Tuna Salad Lettuce Wraps
lunch.description: Light and refreshing tuna salad (made with vegan mayo) served in crisp lettuce cups.
ingredients: 4 large lettuce leaves (e.g., butter or romaine)
Salt and black pepper to taste
lunch.instructions: 1. Drain tuna well. In a bowl, flake the tuna with a fork. 2. Stir in vegan mayonnaise, celery, red onion, parsley, salt, and pepper until well combined. 3. Spoon the tuna salad into the lettuce leaves and serve as wraps.

dinner.name: Halal Chicken and Vegetable Curry with Brown Rice
dinner.description: A fragrant and hearty curry with tender Halal chicken and mixed vegetables in a spiced coconut milk base, served with brown rice.
ingredients: 15g Halal certified curry paste (ensure no shrimp paste or alcohol)
dinner.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat. Add onion and cook until softened. Add garlic, ginger, and curry paste, cook for 1 minute until fragrant. 2. Add cubed chicken and cook until lightly browned. 3. Pour in coconut milk, add bell pepper and green beans. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender. 4. Season with salt to taste. 5. Serve the chicken curry over cooked brown rice.

snack.name: Mixed Berries
snack.description: A simple bowl of fresh, sweet, and antioxidant-rich mixed berries.
snack.instructions: Enjoy as a refreshing snack.

breakfast.name: Tofu Scramble with Wholemeal Toast
breakfast.description: A flavorful plant-based scramble made from firm tofu, seasoned with turmeric for color, served with wholemeal toast.
ingredients: 150g firm tofu, pressed and crumbled (Halal certified)
Salt and black pepper to taste
breakfast.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat. Add onion and cook until softened. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds. 2. Add crumbled tofu, turmeric, nutritional yeast, salt, and pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 5-7 minutes until heated through and slightly golden. 3. Stir in fresh spinach until wilted. 4. Toast the wholemeal bread. 5. Serve tofu scramble alongside the toast.

lunch.name: Leftover Halal Chicken and Vegetable Curry
lunch.description: Enjoy the delicious leftovers from Friday's hearty Halal chicken and vegetable curry.
ingredients: Portion of Halal Chicken and Vegetable Curry from Friday (approx. 350g)
lunch.instructions: Reheat the curry and brown rice until piping hot. Serve.

dinner.name: Halal Beef Burger (bunless) with Sweet Potato Fries
dinner.description: A juicy Halal beef patty served bunless with a side of homemade sweet potato fries and fresh salad.
ingredients: Salt, black pepper, paprika to taste
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Toss sweet potato fries with olive oil, salt, pepper, and paprika. Spread on a baking sheet and bake for 25-30 minutes, flipping halfway, until crispy. 2. Season beef patty with salt and pepper. Cook in a pan or grill over medium-high heat for 4-5 minutes per side, or until cooked to your preference. 3. Serve the bunless beef patty with sweet potato fries, mixed greens, tomato, onion, and a side of ketchup.

snack.name: Cucumber Sticks with Hummus
snack.description: Crisp cucumber sticks paired with creamy hummus for a light and refreshing snack.
snack.instructions: Serve cucumber sticks with hummus for dipping.

breakfast.name: Breakfast Smoothie Bowl
breakfast.description: A thick, creamy smoothie bowl topped with granola, seeds, and fresh fruit.
breakfast.instructions: 1. Blend frozen berries, frozen banana, and almond milk until smooth and thick. If too thick, add a splash more almond milk. 2. Pour into a bowl. 3. Top with granola, chia seeds, and fresh kiwi slices.

lunch.name: Spicy Halal Chicken Wrap
lunch.description: Grilled Halal chicken breast with a hint of spice, wrapped in a whole wheat tortilla with fresh veggies.
ingredients: 100g Halal chicken breast, grilled and sliced
15g sriracha sauce (ensure Halal certified, no alcohol)
lunch.instructions: 1. In a small bowl, mix sriracha sauce and vegan mayonnaise. 2. Lay out the tortilla. Spread the spicy mayo mixture evenly. 3. Layer with mixed greens, sliced bell pepper, red onion, and grilled chicken. 4. Roll up the tortilla tightly and slice in half if desired.

dinner.name: Halal Fish Tacos with Cabbage Slaw
dinner.description: Flaky baked white fish in soft corn tortillas, topped with a crisp cabbage slaw and a light lime dressing.
ingredients: 180g white fish fillet (e.g., cod, tilapia, Halal certified)
Salt and black pepper to taste
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Place fish on a baking sheet, drizzle with 5ml olive oil, chili powder, salt, and pepper. Bake for 12-15 minutes until fish flakes easily. 2. While fish bakes, prepare slaw: In a bowl, combine shredded red cabbage, shredded carrot, and chopped cilantro. Whisk together lime juice, 5ml olive oil, salt, and pepper for the dressing. Pour over slaw and toss to combine. 3. Warm tortillas according to package instructions (microwave or dry pan). 4. Flake the cooked fish. Assemble tacos by placing flaked fish in tortillas and topping with cabbage slaw.

snack.name: Hard-boiled Egg
snack.description: A simple and protein-rich hard-boiled egg.
snack.instructions: Peel and enjoy.

craving_alternatives.instructions: 1. Place frozen banana slices and almond milk in a food processor or high-speed blender. 2. Blend until smooth and creamy, like soft-serve ice cream. Add cocoa powder if desired for a chocolate version. 3. Serve immediately.

1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). 2. Toss frozen edamame with olive oil, chili powder, garlic powder, and salt. 3. Spread in a single layer on a baking sheet. 4. Roast for 15-20 minutes, stirring halfway, until lightly browned and slightly crispy.

proteins: 180g Halal white fish fillet (e.g., cod, tilapia)
1 can (140g) Halal certified tuna in water
pantry: Halal certified curry paste (no shrimp paste or alcohol)
Sriracha sauce (ensure Halal certified, no alcohol)
spices: Halal certified tagine spice mix (if not making your own blend)
Everything but the bagel seasoning (ensure Halal certified, no alcohol-containing oils)

breakfast.name: Scrambled Eggs with Spinach and Whole Wheat Toast
breakfast.description: Fluffy scrambled eggs with wilted spinach served with a slice of whole wheat toast.
ingredients: salt and black pepper to taste
breakfast.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat. 2. Add spinach and cook until wilted, about 1-2 minutes. 3. Whisk eggs with salt and pepper. Pour into the pan with spinach. 4. Scramble until cooked through. 5. Serve with toasted whole wheat bread.

lunch.name: Chicken & Veggie Quinoa Bowl (Middle Eastern Inspired)
lunch.description: Grilled halal chicken breast with quinoa, roasted vegetables, and a lemon-tahini dressing.
ingredients: salt and black pepper to taste
lunch.instructions: 1. Cut chicken breast into bite-sized pieces and season with salt, pepper, and a pinch of cumin. Grill or pan-fry until cooked through. 2. Chop zucchini and bell pepper. Toss with 5 grams olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast in oven at 200°C for 15-20 minutes until tender. 3. Prepare lemon-tahini dressing by whisking tahini, lemon juice, water, salt, and pepper until smooth. 4. Assemble the bowl with quinoa, roasted vegetables, and grilled chicken. Drizzle with dressing.

dinner.name: Baked Salmon with Asparagus
dinner.description: Flaky baked salmon fillet served with tender roasted asparagus.
ingredients: salt and black pepper to taste
dinner.instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C. Line a baking tray with parchment paper. 2. Place salmon on one side of the tray. Drizzle with half the olive oil, lemon juice, salt, pepper, and a sprinkle of garlic powder. 3. Toss asparagus with the remaining olive oil, salt, and pepper, then place on the other side of the tray. 4.
