exercises.name: Plank with Band Around Wrists
exercises.description: Assume a plank position, band around wrists. Hold position, keeping body in a straight line. Avoid letting hips sag or rise.
exercises.easier: Perform on knees or reduce hold time
exercises.harder: Hold for longer or add slight shoulder taps

exercises.name: Band Squat Jumps
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding handles at shoulder height. Perform a squat, then explosively jump upwards. Land softly and go into the next squat.
exercises.easier: Perform regular band squats, focusing on speed
exercises.harder: Increase height of jump

exercises.name: Band Overhead Press
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding handles at shoulder height, palms forward. Press hands straight overhead. Control the descent.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce range of motion
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or increase reps

exercises.name: Band Bicep Curls
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding handles with palms up. Keeping elbows tucked, curl hands towards shoulders. Lower with control.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce range of motion
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or increase reps

exercises.name: Band Wood Chops (High to Low)
exercises.description: Anchor band overhead to one side. Stand facing anchor, holding handle. Pull band diagonally across your body, rotating torso. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Reduce range of motion or use a lighter band
exercises.harder: Increase range of motion or use a heavier band

exercises.name: Band Walks (forward/backward)
exercises.description: Place band around ankles. Take controlled steps forward and backward, maintaining a slight squat. Focus on activation and blood flow.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or no band
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or maintain deeper squat

exercises.name: Band Pull-Aparts
exercises.description: Hold band, arms extended, pull apart squeezing shoulder blades. Focus on posture.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band
exercises.harder: Perform with a slightly wider grip

exercises.name: Walking (brisk)
exercises.description: Brisk walk to increase heart rate and promote recovery.
exercises.easier: Slower pace
exercises.harder: Faster pace or incorporate inclines

plan_data.plan_name: 4-Week Resistance Band Fat Loss & Muscle Build
plan_data.overview: A progressive 4-week full-body workout plan for intermediate individuals, focusing on weight loss and muscle building using only resistance bands. Designed to be mindful of lower back health and incorporate progressive overload.

weeks.description: Establish proper form and engagement with resistance band exercises. Focus on controlled movements and activating target muscles. Lower volume to ease into the routine.

exercises.name: Band Squats
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding ends at shoulder height. Perform a regular squat, ensuring knees track over toes and back is straight. Focus on pushing through heels.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or perform bodyweight squats.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or add a 2-second hold at the bottom of each squat.

exercises.name: Band Glute Bridges
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat. Place a resistance band around your thighs, just above the knees. Drive hips up, squeezing glutes at the top, pushing knees slightly out against the band. Keep lower back neutral.
exercises.easier: Perform without a resistance band or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Perform single-leg glute bridges or hold the top position for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Band Chest Press (Floor)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, band wrapped under your upper back. Hold ends with hands, elbows on floor at 45 degrees. Press hands straight up, squeezing chest. Keep feet flat on floor.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or decrease rest between sets.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Aparts
exercises.description: Stand tall, holding a resistance band with both hands extended in front of you at shoulder height, palms down. Pull the band apart, squeezing shoulder blades together, until arms are out to the sides. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or grip wider.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band, grip narrower, or perform with arms slightly elevated.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.description: Hold a straight line from head to heels, supporting yourself on forearms and toes. Keep core tight, glutes squeezed, and avoid sagging or arching the lower back.
exercises.easier: Perform plank on knees or hold for a shorter duration.
exercises.harder: Lift one leg slightly off the floor or perform on hands.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Walks
exercises.description: Place a resistance band around your ankles or just above your knees. Stand with feet hip-width apart, slight bend in knees, and chest up. Step sideways, maintaining tension on the band. Take 10 steps one direction, then 10 steps back.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or place it higher on the thighs.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or perform in a deeper squat stance.

exercises.name: Band Donkey Kicks
exercises.description: Start on all fours. Loop a band around one foot and hold the ends in your hands. Keeping a neutral spine, kick one leg back and up, squeezing the glute at the top. Control the return. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or perform without a band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or add a pulse at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Band Overhead Press (Seated)
exercises.description: Sit on a chair or floor, band under your glutes. Hold ends in each hand, hands at shoulder height, palms forward. Press arms overhead, extending fully. Control the descent. Maintain a straight back.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or perform standing with feet on band for more tension.

exercises.name: Bird Dog
exercises.description: Start on all fours, hands under shoulders, knees under hips. Extend one arm forward and the opposite leg straight back, maintaining a flat back and stable core. Return to start. Alternate sides.
exercises.easier: Perform only arm or leg extension separately.
exercises.harder: Hold a light resistance band between hand and foot for added tension or hold the extended position for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Band Rows (Standing)
exercises.description: Stand on the center of the band with feet hip-width apart. Hold an end in each hand. Hinge slightly at the hips, keeping a straight back. Pull the band towards your torso, squeezing shoulder blades. Control the release.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or grip higher on the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or grip lower on the band to increase tension.

exercises.name: Band Bicep Curls
exercises.description: Stand on the center of the band, feet hip-width apart. Hold an end in each hand, palms facing forward. Keep elbows tucked to sides and curl hands towards shoulders, squeezing biceps. Control the release.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or stand with feet narrower.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or stand with feet wider to increase tension.

exercises.name: Band Triceps Extensions (Overhead)
exercises.description: Anchor the band under one foot or sit on it. Hold the other end with both hands behind your head, elbows pointed forward. Extend arms overhead, straightening elbows. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or perform single-arm extensions.

exercises.name: Pallof Press (Kneeling)
exercises.description: Kneel perpendicular to an anchored band (wrap around a sturdy object). Grab the band with both hands at chest height. Press the band straight out in front of you, resisting rotation. Hold for a second, then control the return. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or stand instead of kneel.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or perform standing with feet closer together.

exercises.name: Band Good Mornings
exercises.description: Stand on the band with feet hip-width apart, holding ends at your neck/shoulders. Keeping a slight bend in knees and a flat back, hinge at the hips, pushing glutes back until you feel a stretch in hamstrings. Return by squeezing glutes. Focus on hip hinge, not spinal flexion.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or perform bodyweight only. Focus heavily on hip hinge mechanics.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or increase range of motion with perfect form.

exercises.name: Band Reverse Lunges
exercises.description: Step on the middle of the band with one foot, holding the ends at shoulder height. Step back with the other leg into a lunge, lowering until both knees are bent at 90 degrees. Push through front heel to return. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform bodyweight reverse lunges.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or add a slight pause at the bottom.

exercises.name: Band Chest Fly (Floor)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, band wrapped under your upper back. Hold ends with hands, arms extended to the sides with a slight bend in elbows. Bring hands together above your chest, squeezing. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or slow down the eccentric (lowering) phase.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent at 90 degrees, arms extended towards the ceiling. Lower one arm and the opposite leg towards the floor simultaneously, keeping lower back pressed into the floor. Return and alternate.
exercises.easier: Only extend one limb at a time (arm or leg).
exercises.harder: Place a small resistance band around your feet for added tension or hold light weights in hands.

exercises.name: Band Assisted Hamstring Stretch
exercises.description: Lie on your back, loop a band around the arch of one foot. Extend that leg towards the ceiling, using the band to gently pull it closer to your head until you feel a comfortable stretch in your hamstring. Hold.
exercises.easier: Don't pull as far, keep knee slightly bent.
exercises.harder: Pull further, ensuring lower back stays flat, try to straighten knee fully.

exercises.name: Band Assisted Quad Stretch
exercises.description: Lie on your side or stomach. Loop the band around your ankle. Grab the other end of the band and gently pull your heel towards your glutes until you feel a comfortable stretch in your quad and hip flexor. Hold.
exercises.easier: Don't pull as far, keep the stretch gentle.
exercises.harder: Increase the depth of the stretch, ensuring no discomfort in the knee.

exercises.name: Thoracic Mobility with Band
exercises.description: Kneel facing an anchor point. Loop a band around it and hold with both hands. With straight arms, hinge at your hips, letting the band pull your arms forward, stretching your lats and upper back. Focus on extending through your thoracic spine. Return to start.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Increase the stretch by hinging deeper or holding the stretch longer.

weeks.description: Slightly increase sets/reps or active time. Focus on maintaining strong form with increased effort. Introduce new variations to challenge muscles differently.

exercises.name: Band Squat to Press
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding ends at shoulder height. Perform a squat, then as you stand up, press the band overhead. Control the movement both ways.
exercises.easier: Separate the movements: squat, then press. Or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or add a slight pause at the bottom of the squat.

exercises.name: Band Elevated Glute Bridges
exercises.description: Place band around thighs. Elevate your feet on a low step or sturdy books. Drive hips up, squeezing glutes at the top, pushing knees slightly out against the band.
exercises.easier: Perform regular glute bridges (feet on floor) or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Perform single-leg elevated glute bridges or hold the top for 3 seconds.

exercises.name: Band Push-Ups
exercises.description: Loop a resistance band across your upper back, holding the ends in your hands under your chest. Perform push-ups. The band will add resistance as you push up.
exercises.easier: Perform push-ups on your knees without a band or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or slow down the eccentric phase.

exercises.name: Band Face Pulls
exercises.muscle_group: Rear Delts, Upper Back, Rotator Cuff
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band at chest height. Grab the band with both hands, palms facing each other. Step back to create tension. Pull the band towards your face, leading with your elbows and externally rotating your shoulders. Squeeze shoulder blades.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or take a smaller step back.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or add a 1-second hold at the peak contraction.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.description: Support yourself on one forearm and the side of your foot, keeping your body in a straight line. Keep hips lifted and core engaged. Hold for the duration, then switch sides.
exercises.easier: Perform with knees bent or hold for a shorter duration.
exercises.harder: Add a hip dip or raise the top leg while holding the plank.

exercises.name: Band Monster Walks
exercises.description: Place a resistance band around your ankles or just above your knees. Stand with feet hip-width apart, slight bend in knees, and chest up. Take diagonal steps forward and out, like a monster, maintaining tension on the band. Take 10 steps one direction, then 10 steps back.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or place it higher on the thighs.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or perform with shorter, quicker steps.

exercises.name: Band Romanian Deadlifts (RDLs)
exercises.description: Stand on the band, feet hip-width apart, holding ends in hands. Keep a slight bend in knees and a straight back. Hinge at the hips, pushing glutes back, lowering torso until you feel a stretch in hamstrings. Return by squeezing glutes. Focus on the hip hinge, not rounding your back.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or slow down the eccentric phase.

exercises.name: Band Kickbacks (Standing)
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band to a stable object at ankle height. Loop the other end around your ankle. Stand tall, slight bend in the supporting knee. Kick the banded leg straight back, squeezing the glute. Control the return. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or add a pulse at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Band Anti-Rotation Press (Standing)
exercises.description: Stand perpendicular to an anchored band (wrap around a sturdy object). Grab the band with both hands at chest height. Step out to create tension. Press the band straight out in front of you, resisting rotation. Hold for a second, then control the return. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or stand closer to the anchor point.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or stand with feet closer together.

exercises.name: Band Bent-Over Rows
exercises.description: Stand on the band with feet hip-width apart. Hinge at the hips, keeping your back straight and nearly parallel to the floor (ensure no lower back discomfort). Pull the band towards your chest, squeezing shoulder blades. Control the release.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or stand more upright.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or slow down the eccentric phase.

exercises.name: Band Hammer Curls
exercises.description: Stand on the center of the band, feet hip-width apart. Hold an end in each hand, palms facing each other (hammer grip). Keep elbows tucked and curl hands towards shoulders. Control the release.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or stand with feet narrower.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or stand with feet wider to increase tension.

exercises.name: Band Triceps Pushdowns
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band high (e.g., around a closed door or sturdy post). Grab the end of the band with both hands, palms down. Keep elbows tucked to sides and press the band straight down, extending triceps fully. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or slow down the eccentric phase.

exercises.name: Band Upright Rows
exercises.description: Stand on the band with feet hip-width apart, holding ends in hands close together. Pull the band upwards towards your chin, leading with elbows. Keep the band close to your body. Control the descent.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or add a slight pause at the top.

exercises.name: Band Squat Jumps
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding ends at shoulder height. Perform a squat, then explode upwards into a jump, landing softly. Repeat quickly.
exercises.easier: Perform regular band squats without the jump.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or increase jump height.

exercises.name: Band Push-Up (Explosive)
exercises.description: Loop a resistance band across your upper back. Perform a push-up, then explode upwards to briefly lift your hands off the floor. Land softly and control the descent.
exercises.easier: Perform regular band push-ups or push-ups from knees.
exercises.harder: Increase the band resistance or clap hands in mid-air.

exercises.name: Band Plank with Leg Lift
exercises.description: Get into a plank position. Place a small loop band around your ankles. While maintaining core stability, lift one leg straight up a few inches, resisting the band. Alternate legs.
exercises.easier: Perform a regular plank without leg lifts or band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or hold the leg lift for 1-2 seconds.

exercises.name: Band Standing Oblique Twists
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band to a sturdy object at chest height. Stand perpendicular to the anchor, holding the band with both hands. Rotate your torso away from the anchor, extending arms, engaging obliques. Control the return. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or slow down the return phase.

exercises.name: Band Assisted Pectoral Stretch
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band to a stable object at chest height. Stand facing away from the anchor, holding the band with one hand, arm extended to the side. Gently step forward and rotate your torso away from the arm, feeling a stretch in your chest. Hold, then switch sides.
exercises.easier: Step forward less, use lighter tension.
exercises.harder: Increase the stretch by stepping further or holding for longer.

exercises.name: Band Assisted Lat Stretch
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band high. Grab the band with one hand and step back, letting the band pull your arm overhead. Side bend away from the band, feeling a stretch down your side. Hold, then switch sides.
exercises.easier: Reduce the tension or step back less.
exercises.harder: Deepen the side bend and hold for a longer duration.

exercises.name: Cat-Cow with Band Assistance
exercises.description: On all fours, place a light resistance band around your upper back. Gently arch your back (cow pose), then round your back (cat pose), letting the band provide light resistance or assistance to deepen the movement. Focus on spinal articulation.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band, focusing on controlled movement.
exercises.harder: Slow down the movement significantly, pausing at peak extension/flexion.

weeks.description: Increase intensity through higher reps/sets or by introducing more challenging exercise variations. Focus on progressive overload and pushing your limits safely.

exercises.name: Band Squats (Pulse at Bottom)
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding ends at shoulder height. Perform a squat, then at the bottom, perform two small pulses before driving back up. Keep constant tension on the band.
exercises.easier: Perform regular band squats without the pulse.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or increase the number of pulses.

exercises.name: Band Single-Leg RDLs
exercises.description: Stand on the band with one foot, holding ends in hands. Keep a slight bend in the standing knee. Hinge at the hip, extending the free leg straight back for balance, lowering torso until you feel a stretch. Return by squeezing glutes. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform with both feet on the band (regular RDL) or without a band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or increase the hold at the bottom.

exercises.name: Band Incline Chest Press (Floor)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, band wrapped under your mid-back. Hold ends with hands, elbows on floor at a higher angle (approx. 60 degrees). Press hands up and slightly inward, focusing on the upper chest. Keep feet flat on floor.
exercises.easier: Perform regular flat band chest press or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or slow the eccentric phase to 3 seconds.

exercises.name: Band High Rows
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band at shoulder height. Grab the band with both hands. Pull the band towards your upper chest, elbows pointing out and high. Squeeze shoulder blades. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or perform standing rows (pulling lower).
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or add a 1-second hold at peak contraction.

exercises.name: Plank with Alternating Arm Reach
exercises.description: Start in a plank position. Keeping your hips stable, reach one arm straight forward, hold briefly, then return. Alternate arms. Avoid rocking the hips.
exercises.easier: Perform regular plank or reach shorter.
exercises.harder: Hold the reach longer or add a light band around wrists for resistance.

exercises.name: Band Squat Walks (Forward/Backward)
exercises.description: Place a resistance band around your ankles or just above knees. Get into a quarter-squat position. Take small, controlled steps forward, then backward, maintaining the squat and tension on the band.
exercises.easier: Perform regular band lateral walks or stand more upright.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or squat deeper during the walks.

exercises.name: Band Walking Lunges
exercises.description: Stand on the band with one foot, holding ends at shoulder height. Step forward with the other leg into a lunge, bringing the back knee almost to the floor. Push through the front heel and bring the back foot forward to meet the front, then step with the other leg. Walk forward.
exercises.easier: Perform stationary band lunges or walking lunges without a band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or increase the step length.

exercises.name: Band Fire Hydrants
exercises.description: Start on all fours. Place a resistance band around your thighs, just above the knees. Keeping a 90-degree bend in the knee, lift one leg out to the side like a dog at a fire hydrant, pushing against the band. Keep hips stable. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or add a pulse at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Band Leg Press (Lying)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent. Loop a strong resistance band around both feet and hold the ends in your hands by your chest. Push your feet away, extending legs against the band's resistance, keeping lower back pressed down. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or perform with one leg at a time.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or decrease rest between sets.

exercises.name: Band Renegade Rows (Modified)
exercises.description: Start in a high plank position with feet wide. Place one hand on the band (anchored under it or to a stable object). Pull the band up towards your rib cage, keeping your core tight and hips stable. Control the return. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform standing single-arm rows or from knees.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or reduce foot width for increased instability.

exercises.name: Band Concentrated Curls
exercises.description: Sit on a chair, feet flat. Loop the band under one foot, hold the end in the same hand. Place elbow on the inner thigh for support. Curl the band up, squeezing the bicep. Focus on isolation. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or remove thigh support.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or slow down the eccentric phase (3 seconds).

exercises.name: Band Overhead Triceps Extension (Single Arm)
exercises.description: Anchor the band under one foot or sit on it. Hold the other end with one hand, arm extended overhead, elbow pointed forward. Extend arm overhead, straightening elbow. Control the return. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or perform with two hands.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or decrease rest between sets.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Raises
exercises.description: Stand on the band with feet together, holding ends in each hand. Keep a slight bend in elbows. Raise arms out to the sides until they are parallel with the floor, squeezing shoulders. Control the descent. Avoid shrugging.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or perform one arm at a time.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or add a 1-second hold at the top.

exercises.name: Band Thrusters
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding ends at shoulder height. Perform a squat, then explode up, using the momentum to drive the band overhead. Control the descent into the next squat.
exercises.easier: Perform separate band squats and band overhead presses.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or move through the full range of motion faster.

exercises.name: Band Mountain Climbers
exercises.description: Start in a high plank position. Place a small loop band around your feet or ankles. Alternately bring one knee towards your chest, then switch, maintaining core stability. The band will add resistance.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or slow down the movement.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or speed up the movement while maintaining form.

exercises.name: Band Jumping Jacks
exercises.description: Place a small loop band around your ankles. Perform jumping jacks, ensuring you keep tension on the band as you spread your legs. Arms still go overhead.
exercises.easier: Perform regular jumping jacks without a band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or add a squat at the bottom of each jack.

exercises.name: Band Russian Twists
exercises.description: Sit on the floor, knees bent, feet elevated or on floor. Hold a resistance band with both hands, extending it straight out. Twist torso from side to side, bringing the band towards the floor beside your hip. Maintain a strong, upright core.
exercises.easier: Keep feet on the floor or perform without a band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or keep feet elevated and twist faster.

exercises.name: Figure Four Stretch with Band
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent. Cross one ankle over the opposite knee. Loop a resistance band around the thigh of the bottom leg. Gently pull the band to draw the bottom knee towards your chest, deepening the stretch in the glute of the crossed leg. Hold, then switch sides.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or pull less intensely.
exercises.harder: Increase the intensity of the pull, ensuring comfort.

exercises.name: Band Assisted Ankle Mobility
exercises.description: Sit on the floor, extend one leg. Loop a resistance band around the ball of your foot, holding ends in your hands. Flex and point your foot, using the band to gently increase the range of motion. Circle your ankle. Perform on both sides.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band, focusing on controlled movement.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or increase the range of motion and hold each position briefly.

exercises.name: Thread the Needle with Band
exercises.description: Start on all fours. Place a light resistance band under one hand. Thread the other arm under your body, palm up, until your shoulder and head rest on the floor, letting the band provide gentle tension. Hold, then push up and repeat on the other side.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band.
exercises.harder: Increase the reach and hold the stretch longer.

weeks.description: Peak week for intensity. Focus on challenging yourself with higher reps/sets, shorter rests, or more complex movements. Maintain excellent form under fatigue.

exercises.name: Band Jump Squats (Explosive)
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding ends at shoulder height. Perform a deep squat, then explode upwards for maximum height, landing softly. Focus on power and quick transitions.
exercises.easier: Perform band squats with a pulse, or reduce jump height.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or decrease rest time between sets.

exercises.name: Band Single-Leg Glute Bridges (with hold)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat. Place a resistance band around your thighs. Lift one leg straight up. Drive hips up with the standing leg, squeezing glutes at the top and holding for 2 seconds, pushing knee out against the band. Keep lower back neutral. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform two-leg glute bridges or no band.
exercises.harder: Increase the hold time to 3 seconds or increase band resistance.

exercises.name: Band Plyometric Push-Ups
exercises.description: Loop a resistance band across your upper back. Perform a push-up, then explode upwards to briefly lift your hands off the floor (a clap is optional). Land softly and control the descent. Focus on speed and power.
exercises.easier: Perform regular band push-ups or from knees.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or try to elevate your hands higher off the floor.

exercises.name: Band Plank with Rows
exercises.description: Start in a high plank position with feet wide. Loop a resistance band under one hand, anchoring it to a sturdy object or holding the other end with your non-working hand. Perform a row, pulling the band towards your rib cage, maintaining a stable plank. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform regular plank or kneeling rows.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or narrow your foot stance.

exercises.name: Band Triceps Dips (Assisted)
exercises.description: Sit on the floor, hands behind you on a sturdy elevated surface (chair/step), fingers pointing forward. Loop a band around your knees. Lift your glutes off the floor, then lower your body by bending elbows. Push back up. The band around the knees adds resistance. Focus on driving up with triceps.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or with feet closer to your hands.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or extend legs further out.

exercises.name: Band Squats (2-second pause)
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding ends at shoulder height. Perform a squat, pausing for 2 seconds at the bottom before driving back up. Maintain core tightness throughout.
exercises.easier: Perform regular band squats without a pause.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or increase the pause duration.

exercises.name: Band Curtsy Lunges
exercises.description: Stand on the band with one foot, holding ends at shoulder height. Step one leg back and across, as if curtsying, lowering until the back knee is close to the floor. Push through the front heel to return. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform regular reverse lunges or without a band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or deepen the lunge.

exercises.name: Band Hip Abduction (Standing)
exercises.description: Loop a resistance band around your ankles. Stand tall, slight bend in supporting knee. Lift one leg out to the side, pushing against the band. Keep torso upright and hips level. Control the return. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or hold onto a wall for balance.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or add a pulse at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Band Hamstring Curls (Lying)
exercises.description: Lie on your stomach. Anchor a resistance band to a sturdy object. Loop the other end around one ankle. Curl your heel towards your glutes, squeezing your hamstring. Control the return. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or decrease rest between sets.

exercises.name: Band Bent-Over Rows (Pause at top)
exercises.description: Stand on the band with feet hip-width apart. Hinge at the hips, keeping your back straight. Pull the band towards your chest, squeezing shoulder blades and holding for 2 seconds at the top. Control the release.
exercises.easier: Perform regular band bent-over rows without a pause.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or increase the pause duration.

exercises.name: Band Bicep Curls (21s)
exercises.description: Perform 7 reps from bottom to half-way, then 7 reps from half-way to top, then 7 full range reps. Stand on the center of the band, holding ends. Keep elbows tucked. This is one set.
exercises.easier: Perform regular band bicep curls.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or slow down the tempo on all reps.

exercises.name: Band Triceps Kickbacks
exercises.description: Stand on the band with one foot, holding the other end in one hand. Hinge at the hips, keeping back straight. Arm tucked at your side, elbow bent 90 degrees. Extend arm straight back, squeezing triceps. Control the return. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or add a 1-second hold at peak contraction.

exercises.name: Band Shoulder Press (Standing, Single Arm)
exercises.description: Stand on the band with one foot, holding the end in the same hand. Hand at shoulder height, palm forward. Press arm overhead, extending fully. Control the descent. Maintain a straight back and tight core to resist rotation. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or perform seated with two hands.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or decrease rest between sets.

exercises.name: Band Burpees (Modified)
exercises.description: Start standing. Squat down and place hands on the floor. Jump feet back into a plank. Perform a band push-up (if using one). Jump feet back to hands. Stand up, optionally adding a small jump. No overhead jump to avoid lower back strain. Loop band around upper back for push-up resistance if desired.
exercises.easier: Step back into plank and step forward. Perform push-up on knees or omit.
exercises.harder: Speed up the movement, add a stronger band for push-up.

exercises.name: Band Frog Jumps
exercises.description: Place a loop band around your ankles. Start in a low squat position, hands on the floor. Explode forward in a jump, landing softly back in a squat. Maintain tension on the band. Focus on powerful, controlled jumps.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or jump shorter.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or increase jump distance.

exercises.name: Band Inchworm with Push-Up
exercises.description: Start standing. Hinge at hips and walk hands out to a plank position. Perform a band push-up (band around upper back). Walk hands back to feet, standing up. Repeat. Focus on core stability and smooth movement.
exercises.easier: Omit the push-up or perform push-up on knees.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band for the push-up or slow down the walk for more control.

exercises.name: Band Bicycle Crunches
exercises.description: Lie on your back, hands behind head, legs extended and slightly elevated. Place a small loop band around your feet. Bring one elbow towards the opposite knee while extending the other leg, maintaining core engagement. Alternate sides rapidly.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or keep feet closer to the floor.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or slow down the movement for more control and tension.

exercises.name: Full Body Band Dynamic Stretch Circuit
exercises.description: Perform a continuous flow of light band stretches: start with a band assisted hamstring sweep, then transition to a dynamic hip flexor stretch with band, then a banded chest opener. Focus on gentle movement and deep breathing, not static holds.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or simplify the transitions.
exercises.harder: Focus on deeper ranges of motion and smoother transitions between movements.

exercises.name: Band Assisted Deep Squat Hold
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band low. Stand facing the anchor, holding the band with both hands. Use the band to gently pull you into a deep squat position, helping to maintain balance and depth. Hold comfortably, focusing on hip and ankle mobility.
exercises.easier: Hold onto a stable object for support, or don't use the band.
exercises.harder: Deepen the squat and try to maintain form with less assistance from the band.

exercises.name: Band Shoulder Dislocates (Light)
exercises.description: Hold a very light resistance band with a wide grip. Start with the band in front of your body. Keeping arms straight, slowly bring the band overhead and behind your back, then return. Ensure no pain, only a gentle stretch. Adjust grip width as needed.
exercises.easier: Use a wider grip or perform with a stick/broom handle instead of a band.
exercises.harder: Use a narrower grip, ensuring smooth movement and no pain.

plan_data.plan_name: 4-Week Beginner Resistance Band Full Body Plan
plan_data.overview: A progressive 4-week plan designed for beginners to build general fitness and health using resistance bands. Focuses on balanced full-body strength and endurance, gradually increasing intensity.

weeks.description: Getting acquainted with resistance band movements. Prioritize correct form and controlled movement. Light to moderate band resistance.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Squats
exercises.description: Place a resistance band just above your knees. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Perform a squat, pushing knees out against the band. Keep chest up.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or no band
exercises.harder: Hold the squat at the bottom for 2-3 seconds

exercises.name: Resistance Band Glute Bridges
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat. Place a resistance band just above your knees. Drive through your heels to lift hips off the floor, squeezing glutes. Push knees out against the band.
exercises.easier: Perform without a resistance band
exercises.harder: Hold at the top for 2 seconds, then slowly lower

exercises.name: Resistance Band Push-Ups
exercises.description: Loop a long resistance band around your back and hold the ends under your hands in a plank position. Perform a push-up, lowering your chest and pushing back up against the band's resistance.
exercises.easier: Perform push-ups on your knees without the band
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or slow the eccentric phase

exercises.name: Resistance Band Rows (Standing)
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band to a stable object or step on it. Hold the ends, lean slightly forward with a straight back, and pull the band towards your torso, squeezing shoulder blades.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or pause at the top of the pull

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.description: Hold a straight line from head to heels, supporting yourself on forearms and toes. Keep your core tight and don't let your hips sag or rise too high.
exercises.easier: Perform plank on your knees
exercises.harder: Lift one leg off the ground for part of the hold

exercises.name: Resistance Band Lateral Walks
exercises.description: Place a resistance band around your ankles or just above knees. Get into a slight squat stance. Take small, controlled steps sideways, keeping tension on the band.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or place it above the knees
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or place it around the feet

exercises.name: Resistance Band Overhead Press
exercises.description: Stand on a long resistance band with one or both feet. Hold the ends at shoulder height, palms facing forward. Press the bands overhead until arms are fully extended.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or press one arm at a time
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or stand on less of the band for more tension

exercises.name: Resistance Band Triceps Extensions
exercises.description: Hold one end of a resistance band behind your head with one hand, letting it hang down your back. Grab the other end with your other hand, elbow pointing up. Extend the arm overhead.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or perform with both hands
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or stand on the band for more tension

exercises.name: Resistance Band Bicep Curls
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a resistance band, feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the ends with an underhand grip, palms facing up. Curl the band up towards your shoulders, squeezing biceps.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or curl one arm at a time
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or shorten the band for more tension

exercises.name: Resistance Band Crunches
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat. Anchor a resistance band behind your head or neck, holding the ends in your hands. Perform a crunch, pulling against the band's resistance.
exercises.easier: Perform crunches without the resistance band
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or hold the crunch for a count at the top

weeks.description: Increased volume and slight increase in resistance. Focus on maintaining form as you challenge yourself more.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Squats
exercises.description: Place a resistance band just above your knees. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Perform a squat, pushing knees out against the band. Keep chest up.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or increase depth

exercises.name: Resistance Band Glute Bridges
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat. Place a resistance band just above your knees. Drive through your heels to lift hips off the floor, squeezing glutes. Push knees out against the band.
exercises.easier: Perform without a resistance band or reduce range of motion
exercises.harder: Hold at the top for 3 seconds or perform single-leg glute bridges

exercises.name: Resistance Band Push-Ups
exercises.description: Loop a long resistance band around your back and hold the ends under your hands in a plank position. Perform a push-up, lowering your chest and pushing back up against the band's resistance.
exercises.easier: Perform push-ups on your knees with the band
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or elevate your feet

exercises.name: Resistance Band Rows (Seated)
exercises.description: Sit on the floor with legs extended, looping a resistance band around your feet. Hold the ends, keep your back straight, and pull the band towards your lower abdomen, squeezing shoulder blades.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or bend knees slightly
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or pull with a slower, more controlled movement

exercises.name: Resistance Band Bicycle Crunches
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet off the floor. Loop a resistance band around your feet. Bring opposite elbow to opposite knee, extending the other leg, simulating pedaling.
exercises.easier: Perform without the resistance band
exercises.harder: Increase the speed and control, ensuring full extension of the non-working leg

exercises.name: Resistance Band Monster Walks
exercises.description: Place a resistance band around your ankles. Get into a slight squat stance. Step forward diagonally with one leg, then follow with the other, maintaining tension. Alternate leading legs.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or band above the knees
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or place band around the feet

exercises.name: Resistance Band Front Raises
exercises.description: Stand on a resistance band with one or both feet. Hold the ends in front of your thighs, palms facing your body. Lift arms straight out in front of you to shoulder height, keeping elbows slightly bent.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or lift one arm at a time
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or hold at the top for 1-2 seconds

exercises.name: Resistance Band Triceps Pushdowns
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band high (e.g., door frame). Stand facing the anchor, grab the band with both hands, elbows tucked to your sides. Extend arms downwards, pushing the band until arms are straight.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or step closer to the anchor
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or step further from the anchor for more tension

exercises.name: Resistance Band Hammer Curls
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a resistance band. Hold the ends with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Curl the band up towards your shoulders, keeping elbows tucked.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or curl one arm at a time
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or shorten the band for more tension

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.description: Lie on your side, supporting yourself on one forearm and the side of your foot. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels. Repeat on the other side.
exercises.easier: Perform side plank on your knees
exercises.harder: Lift your top leg during the hold

weeks.description: Higher reps/sets, potentially stronger resistance bands. Focus on pushing your limits while maintaining excellent form. Incorporating more compound movements.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Squats (with pulse)
exercises.description: Place a resistance band just above your knees. Perform a squat, then at the bottom, pulse up and down slightly for 2-3 counts before standing fully.
exercises.easier: Perform regular resistance band squats
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band and perform slower, more controlled pulses

exercises.name: Resistance Band Romanian Deadlifts (RDL)
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band. Hold the ends, keeping a slight bend in knees. Hinge at your hips, lowering your torso while keeping your back straight. Feel the stretch in hamstrings.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or reduce range of motion
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band and pause at the bottom of the movement

exercises.name: Resistance Band Chest Press (Standing)
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band behind you (e.g., door frame). Hold the ends, step forward slightly, and press the bands straight out in front of your chest. Squeeze chest at the top.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or step closer to the anchor
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or perform on one arm at a time

exercises.name: Resistance Band Lat Pulldowns (Kneeling)
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band high. Kneel on the floor facing the anchor. Grab the band with both hands, arms extended. Pull the band down towards your chest, squeezing shoulder blades.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or sit closer to the anchor
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or pause at the bottom of the pull

exercises.name: Resistance Band Russian Twists
exercises.description: Sit on the floor, knees bent, feet off the ground (or heels down for easier). Hold a resistance band with both hands, anchored under feet or around an object. Twist torso side to side, keeping core engaged.
exercises.easier: Keep heels on the ground or perform without a band
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or increase the speed with control

exercises.name: Resistance Band Squat Jumps
exercises.description: Place a resistance band just above your knees. Perform a squat and then explode upwards into a jump, landing softly back into a squat. Maintain tension on the band.
exercises.easier: Perform regular resistance band squats
exercises.harder: Increase jump height and ensure explosive power

exercises.name: Resistance Band Fire Hydrants
exercises.description: Get on all fours. Place a resistance band around your thighs, just above the knees. Keeping the knee bent, lift one leg out to the side like a dog 'marking its territory'. Keep core stable.
exercises.easier: Perform without a resistance band
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band and hold the top for 1-2 seconds

exercises.name: Resistance Band Lateral Raises
exercises.description: Stand on a resistance band. Hold the ends at your sides, palms facing in. Lift arms out to the sides to shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in the elbows. Control the descent.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or perform one arm at a time
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or pause at the top of the movement

exercises.name: Resistance Band Preacher Curls
exercises.description: Sit with your elbow propped on your inner thigh or a chair. Loop a resistance band around your foot and hold the end in the working hand. Perform a bicep curl with strict form.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or perform standing bicep curls
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band and focus on slow, controlled eccentric phase

exercises.name: Resistance Band Kickbacks (Triceps)
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band low (e.g., foot). Lean forward with a straight back, elbow bent and tucked close to your side. Extend your arm straight back, squeezing the triceps.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or pause at the full extension

weeks.description: Peak week for this phase. Aim for higher reps/sets or stronger bands. Push your limits with proper form and control. Consider combining exercises for increased challenge.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Squat to Overhead Press
exercises.description: Stand on a long resistance band. Perform a squat, then as you stand, press the band overhead in one fluid motion. Control the descent back to squat position.
exercises.easier: Separate into resistance band squats and resistance band overhead press
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or increase the tempo of the movement

exercises.name: Resistance Band Glute Bridge Marches
exercises.description: Lie on your back, band above knees. Lift into a glute bridge. From this position, slowly lift one foot off the ground, then alternate. Keep hips stable.
exercises.easier: Perform regular resistance band glute bridges
exercises.harder: Hold each leg lift for 2-3 seconds, maintaining hip stability

exercises.name: Resistance Band Push-Up to Row Combo
exercises.description: Start in a plank with a band looped around your back and ends under your hands. Perform a push-up. At the top, perform a single-arm resistance band row (pulling one hand to your side), then repeat on the other side.
exercises.easier: Perform push-ups on knees and alternate with seated band rows
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band and maintain perfect plank form throughout

exercises.name: Resistance Band Deadlifts
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band, feet hip-width apart. Hinge at the hips and bend knees to grab the band. Keep back straight, chest up, and drive through heels to stand up, squeezing glutes.
exercises.easier: Perform resistance band Romanian deadlifts (RDLs)
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band and increase time under tension on the eccentric

exercises.name: Plank with Resistance Band Leg Lifts
exercises.description: Get into a forearm plank position with a resistance band around your ankles. Keeping your core tight and hips stable, lift one leg straight up a few inches, then lower. Alternate legs.
exercises.easier: Perform a regular forearm plank
exercises.harder: Hold the leg lift for 2 seconds at the top before lowering

exercises.name: Resistance Band Curtsy Lunges
exercises.description: Place a resistance band just above your knees. Step one leg behind and across the other, lowering into a lunge. Keep hips square and push out against the band. Alternate legs.
exercises.easier: Perform bodyweight curtsy lunges without a band
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or hold at the bottom of the lunge

exercises.name: Resistance Band Pull-Aparts
exercises.description: Hold a resistance band with both hands out in front of you at shoulder height, palms down. Pull the band apart by squeezing your shoulder blades together until the band touches your chest.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or reduce the range of motion
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or slow down the eccentric phase

exercises.name: Resistance Band Reverse Flyes
exercises.description: Stand on a resistance band with one or both feet. Hinge at the hips, keeping back straight. Let arms hang, palms facing each other. Lift arms out to the sides, squeezing shoulder blades together.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or perform standing
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band and focus on a deep squeeze at the top

exercises.name: Resistance Band Bicep Curl & Triceps Extension Superset
exercises.description: Perform 10 resistance band bicep curls, immediately followed by 10 resistance band overhead triceps extensions. This counts as one set.
exercises.easier: Perform each exercise separately with full rest in between
exercises.harder: Use heavier bands for both exercises or increase reps to 12 each

exercises.name: Resistance Band Dead Bug
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent at 90 degrees, feet off floor. Hold a resistance band with both hands, anchored under feet or around knees. Extend opposite arm and leg simultaneously, keeping low back pressed to floor.
exercises.easier: Perform without a resistance band
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band and increase the extension range

plan_data.overview: A progressive 4-week plan for beginners, focusing on general fitness and full-body strength using resistance bands. Designed for low motivation, with short, confidence-building workouts to foster consistency and adherence.
plan_data.difficulty_hint: make it easier, low motivation, keep it short

weeks.description: Establish basic movement patterns and proper form with light resistance. Focus on controlled movements and building confidence.

exercises.name: Band Glute Bridge
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat. Place a resistance band around your thighs, just above the knees. Push through your heels, lifting your hips towards the ceiling until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees. Squeeze glutes at the top. Lower slowly. Maintain tension on the band.
exercises.easier: Perform without a resistance band.
exercises.harder: Hold the top position for 2-3 seconds, actively pushing knees out against the band.

exercises.name: Band Chest Press (Floor)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, holding the resistance band behind your back, underneath your armpits, with an end in each hand. Hands are at chest level, palms facing each other, elbows bent. Press your hands straight up towards the ceiling, extending your arms. Control the band as you lower slowly. Keep elbows slightly bent.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or loop the band less tightly.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or pause briefly at the top of the press, squeezing your chest.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
exercises.description: Stand tall, holding a resistance band with both hands, arms extended straight out in front of you at shoulder height. Keep a slight bend in your elbows. Pull the band apart by squeezing your shoulder blades together, bringing your hands out to the sides. Control the return to the starting position.
exercises.easier: Use a longer or lighter resistance band.
exercises.harder: Use a shorter or heavier resistance band, or pause for 1-2 seconds at the widest point.

exercises.name: Band Squats
exercises.description: Stand on a resistance band, feet shoulder-width apart, holding the ends of the band at shoulder height (or crossed over your chest). Perform a squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, keeping your chest up. Lower until thighs are parallel to the floor or as comfortable. Drive through heels to stand.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or perform bodyweight squats.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or lower deeper, maintaining good form.

exercises.name: Band Rows
exercises.description: Sit on the floor with legs extended, looping a resistance band around the soles of your feet. Hold an end in each hand, palms facing each other. Keep your back straight. Pull the band towards your torso, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Control the release. Avoid shrugging shoulders.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or loop the band less tightly.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or pause briefly at the peak contraction.

exercises.name: Band Side Leg Raises
exercises.description: Lie on your side with a resistance band looped around your ankles. Keep your body in a straight line. Slowly lift your top leg straight up towards the ceiling, feeling the tension in your outer hip and glute. Control the movement as you lower it back down. Keep your core engaged.
exercises.easier: Perform without a resistance band.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or hold the top position for 1-2 seconds.

weeks.description: Increase reps slightly to build endurance. Focus on smooth, controlled movements and maintaining tension on the band throughout the exercise.

exercises.name: Band Glute Bridge
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat. Place a resistance band around your thighs. Lift hips towards the ceiling, squeezing glutes. Maintain band tension by pushing knees out slightly. Lower slowly.
exercises.easier: Perform without a resistance band.
exercises.harder: Hold the top position for 2-3 seconds, actively pushing knees out against the band and squeezing your glutes.

exercises.name: Band Chest Press (Floor)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, holding the resistance band behind your back. Press your hands straight up, extending your arms. Control the band as you lower slowly, keeping elbows slightly bent.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or loop the band less tightly.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or slow the lowering phase (eccentric) to 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
exercises.description: Stand tall, holding a resistance band with both hands, arms extended straight out at shoulder height. Pull the band apart by squeezing your shoulder blades together. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a longer or lighter resistance band.
exercises.harder: Use a shorter or heavier resistance band, or pause for 1-2 seconds at the widest point, feeling the contraction in your upper back.

exercises.name: Band Squats
exercises.description: Stand on a resistance band, holding the ends at shoulder height. Perform a squat by pushing hips back and bending knees. Drive through heels to stand. Keep constant tension on the band.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or perform bodyweight squats.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band, slow down the descent, or add a brief pause at the bottom of the squat.

exercises.name: Band Rows
exercises.description: Sit with legs extended, looping a resistance band around your feet. Pull the band towards your torso, squeezing shoulder blades together. Control the release. Keep your back straight.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or loop the band less tightly.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or hold the contraction for 1-2 seconds, focusing on the squeeze in your back.

exercises.name: Band Side Leg Raises
exercises.description: Lie on your side with a resistance band looped around your ankles. Slowly lift your top leg straight up. Control the movement as you lower it. Keep your core engaged and body in a straight line.
exercises.easier: Perform without a resistance band.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or add a pulse at the top of the movement (small up-and-down movements).

weeks.description: Introduce slightly more challenging variations or increase resistance. Focus on maintaining stability and form under increased load.

exercises.name: Band Clamshells
exercises.description: Lie on your side, knees bent at 90 degrees, feet stacked. Place a resistance band around your thighs, just above the knees. Keep feet together and hips stacked. Lift your top knee open, like a clamshell, rotating only at the hip. Push against the band. Control the movement as you close. Repeat on both sides.
exercises.easier: Perform without a resistance band.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or hold the top position for 2 seconds, feeling the outer glute activate.

exercises.name: Band Push-Ups (Kneeling)
exercises.description: Get into a kneeling push-up position. Loop a resistance band across your upper back, holding the ends under your hands. Lower your chest towards the floor, keeping elbows close to your body. Push back up, extending against the band's resistance.
exercises.easier: Perform kneeling push-ups without a band.
exercises.harder: Perform from a full plank position with the band if able, or slow down the descent to 3 seconds.

exercises.name: Band Face Pulls
exercises.muscle_group: Upper Back, Rear Delts, Rotator Cuff
exercises.description: Loop a long resistance band around a sturdy anchor point at chest height. Step back to create tension. Hold the band with an overhand grip, hands close together. Pull the band towards your face, leading with your elbows. Squeeze shoulder blades together. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Step closer to the anchor point to reduce tension.
exercises.harder: Step further back for more tension or use a stronger band. Focus on external rotation at the end of the pull.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Walk
exercises.description: Place a resistance band around your ankles or just above the knees. Stand with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and chest up. Take a controlled step sideways, maintaining tension on the band. Bring the other foot to meet it, keeping constant tension. Take 10 steps in one direction, then 10 steps back.
exercises.easier: Place the band around your thighs or use a lighter resistance band.
exercises.harder: Place the band around your ankles and use a stronger band, or take larger steps.

exercises.name: Band Bicep Curls
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a resistance band, feet hip-width apart. Hold an end in each hand, palms facing forward. Keep elbows tucked to your sides. Curl the band up towards your shoulders, squeezing your biceps. Control the release back to the start.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or stand on the band with only one foot.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or perform slowly, focusing on the muscle contraction throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Band Plank (with leg lift)
exercises.description: Start in a plank position on your forearms, with a resistance band looped around your ankles. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels. Lift one leg a few inches off the floor, keeping your hips stable and level. Lower slowly, then repeat on the other side. Alternate legs for desired reps.
exercises.easier: Perform a standard forearm plank without the leg lift or band.
exercises.harder: Lift the leg higher, or hold the leg lift for 2-3 seconds, focusing on core and glute stability.

weeks.description: Solidify your progress by maintaining good form and challenging yourself with slightly higher reps or more demanding variations. You've built a strong foundation!

exercises.name: Band Clamshells
exercises.description: Lie on your side, knees bent, feet stacked. Band around thighs. Lift top knee open, pushing against the band. Control the close. Focus on glute activation. Repeat on both sides.
exercises.easier: Perform without a resistance band.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band and perform a small pulse at the top of the movement before lowering.

exercises.name: Band Push-Ups (Kneeling)
exercises.description: Kneeling push-up position, band across upper back, ends under hands. Lower chest, keeping elbows close. Push back up against band resistance.
exercises.easier: Perform kneeling push-ups without a band.
exercises.harder: Perform from a full plank position with the band, or extend the band further behind your hands for increased tension.

exercises.name: Band Face Pulls
exercises.muscle_group: Upper Back, Rear Delts, Rotator Cuff
exercises.description: Band looped around an anchor point at chest height. Step back for tension. Pull band towards your face, leading with elbows. Squeeze shoulder blades. Control return.
exercises.easier: Step closer to the anchor point to reduce tension.
exercises.harder: Step further back for more tension or use a stronger band. Hold the contraction for 1-2 seconds, focusing on scapular retraction.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Walk
exercises.description: Band around ankles or above knees. Stand with feet hip-width, knees slightly bent. Take controlled steps sideways, maintaining band tension. Repeat 10 steps each direction.
exercises.easier: Place the band around your thighs or use a lighter resistance band.
exercises.harder: Place the band around your ankles and take wider steps, or use a stronger resistance band.

exercises.name: Band Bicep Curls
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a resistance band, holding ends in hands. Curl band up towards shoulders, squeezing biceps. Control the release. Keep elbows tucked.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter resistance band or stand on the band with only one foot.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier resistance band or try a slow, controlled negative (lowering phase) of 3-4 seconds.

exercises.name: Band Plank (with leg lift)
exercises.description: Forearm plank position, band around ankles. Keep body straight. Lift one leg a few inches, keeping hips stable. Lower slowly, then alternate legs for desired reps.
exercises.easier: Perform a standard forearm plank without the leg lift or band.
exercises.harder: Hold the leg lift for 3 seconds, ensuring no hip rotation, or use a stronger resistance band.

plan_data.overview: A progressive 4-week plan for intermediate individuals, focusing on general fitness and health using resistance bands. The plan emphasizes building consistency and confidence with a balanced full-body approach on two dedicated workout days per week.

weeks.description: This week focuses on mastering basic movement patterns with resistance bands. Prioritize good form and controlled movements. Maintain a gentle, confidence-building pace.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Chest Press
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band behind you (or loop around your back). Hold handles at chest height, then press forward, extending arms. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform one arm at a time.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or increase tension by stepping forward.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Row
exercises.description: Sit on the floor with legs extended, looping a band around your feet. Pull the handles towards your torso, squeezing shoulder blades. Slowly release.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform with a slight pause at the peak contraction.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Overhead Press
exercises.description: Stand on the center of the band with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold handles at shoulder height, then press straight overhead. Control the descent.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce the pressing height.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform with one arm at a time.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Bicep Curl
exercises.description: Stand on the band, feet hip-width. Hold handles palms up. Curl hands towards shoulders, squeezing biceps. Slowly lower.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform with a narrower stance on the band.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or widen your stance on the band for more tension.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Squats
exercises.description: Place a loop band just above your knees. Stand feet shoulder-width. Perform a squat, pushing knees out against the band. Drive through heels to stand.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or squat to a higher surface (e.g., chair).
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or add a slight pause at the bottom of the squat.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Glute Bridge
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat. Place a loop band just above your knees. Drive through heels, lift hips, squeezing glutes. Keep knees pushed out. Lower slowly.
exercises.easier: Remove the band or reduce the height of the hip lift.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or add a 2-second hold at the top of the bridge.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Donkey Kicks
exercises.description: Start on all fours. Loop a resistance band around one foot and hold the other end under your hands. Kick the banded leg straight back and up, squeezing glute. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or with a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or increase the hold at the top.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Lateral Walk
exercises.description: Place a loop band around your ankles or just above knees. Get into a slight squat. Step sideways, maintaining tension on the band. Step an equal number of times in both directions.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or keep a more upright posture.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or squat deeper during the walk.

weeks.description: Increasing the challenge slightly by adding more sets or reps, while maintaining good form. Focus on muscular endurance and consistent effort.

weeks.description: This week introduces a slightly higher intensity, potentially using a heavier band or increasing reps to challenge your muscles further. Maintain focus on quality over quantity.

exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform one arm at a time.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or increase tension by stepping forward.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Overhead Press

exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce the pressing height.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform with one arm at a time.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Triceps Extension
exercises.description: Stand with one foot on the band, hold the other end behind your head with elbows pointing up. Extend arms straight overhead. Control the lowering.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform with two hands.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or stand further on the band for more tension.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Squats
exercises.description: Place a loop band just above your knees. Stand feet shoulder-width. Perform a squat, pushing knees out against the band. Drive through heels to stand.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or squat to a higher surface (e.g., chair).
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or add a slight pause at the bottom of the squat.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Romanian Deadlifts (RDL)
exercises.description: Stand on the band with feet hip-width. Hold handles. Hinge at hips, keeping a slight bend in knees and back straight, lowering torso parallel to floor. Feel stretch in hamstrings. Return by squeezing glutes.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce the depth of the hinge.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or increase the depth and slow the eccentric phase.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Side Leg Raises
exercises.description: Lie on your side with a loop band around your ankles. Keep legs straight and lift the top leg directly upwards, engaging the glute. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or with a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or add a pulse at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Clamshells
exercises.description: Lie on your side, knees bent at 90 degrees, feet stacked. Place a loop band around your knees. Keeping feet together, lift your top knee, rotating from the hip. Control the lowering.
exercises.easier: Remove the band or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or add a hold at the top of the movement.

weeks.description: This final week challenges your full-body strength and endurance. Aim for your best performance, focusing on strong form, full range of motion, and consistent effort.

exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform one arm at a time.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or increase tension by stepping forward.

exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform with a narrower stance on the band.

exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or squat to a higher surface (e.g., chair).

exercises.name: Resistance Band Glute Bridge
