exercises.easier: Remove the band or reduce the height of the hip lift.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or add a 2-second hold at the top of the bridge.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Reverse Lunges
exercises.description: Stand on the band with one foot, hold handles. Step back with the other leg, lowering into a lunge. Drive through the front heel to return. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or step back less deeply.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or increase the depth of the lunge.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Standing Leg Abduction
exercises.description: Loop a band around your ankles. Stand tall, core engaged. Slowly lift one leg out to the side, keeping it straight and avoiding leaning. Control the return. Perform all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold onto a support.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or add a slight pause at the peak contraction.

plan_data.overview: An intermediate 4-week progressive plan designed for weight loss and muscle building using only resistance bands. Focuses on balanced full-body workouts while carefully avoiding lower back strain.

weeks.focus: Building foundational strength and proper form with bands.
weeks.description: Getting accustomed to resistance band exercises. Focus on controlled movements and feeling the muscle engagement without stressing the lower back.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Chest Press (Floor)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, band wrapped around your upper back, handles in hands. Press hands upwards as if doing a dumbbell press. Keep core tight, avoid arching back.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or wrap it once around your back for less tension.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or pause for 2 seconds at the top of the press.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Bent-Over Row
exercises.description: Stand on the band, hinge at hips keeping a flat back (neutral spine). Pull the band handles towards your hips, squeezing shoulder blades. Maintain a stable core.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or stand with feet closer together on the band.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or hold the contraction for 2 seconds at the top.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Bicep Curl
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding handles with palms up. Curl hands towards shoulders, keeping elbows tucked. Control the eccentric phase.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or stand on the band with only one foot.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform hammer curls (palms facing each other).

exercises.name: Resistance Band Triceps Extension (Overhead)
exercises.description: Anchor band under one foot, hold handle with both hands behind head. Extend arms overhead, keeping elbows close to ears. Control the movement.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold the band with one hand instead of two.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform single-arm extensions.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Squats
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding handles at shoulder height. Perform a squat, pushing hips back and down. Drive through heels to stand. Maintain neutral spine.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold the band handles looser.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform a 2-second pause at the bottom of the squat.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Glute Bridge
exercises.description: Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat. Place a loop band above knees. Drive hips up, squeezing glutes. Push knees slightly out against band. Lower slowly.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or use a lighter resistance band.
exercises.harder: Hold the top contraction for 2-3 seconds, or perform single-leg glute bridges.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Lateral Walk
exercises.description: Place a loop band above knees or around ankles. Step sideways, maintaining tension on the band. Keep a slight bend in knees, stay low. Repeat for desired reps each side.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or place the band higher up, around the thighs.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform with the band around your ankles.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Hamstring Curl (Standing)
exercises.description: Loop band around an anchor point and one ankle. Stand facing the anchor, slightly hinge forward, and curl heel towards glutes. Keep core engaged.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or decrease the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or slowly control the eccentric phase (lowering).

exercises.name: Resistance Band Squat to Press
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding handles at shoulder height. Perform a squat, and as you stand up, press the band overhead. Control descent.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band for the press or remove the press component.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or add a jump at the top (Squat Jump to Press).

exercises.name: Resistance Band Pull-Apart
exercises.description: Hold a resistance band with both hands out in front of you at chest height, palms down. Pull the band apart, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Maintain a slight bend in elbows.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or grip the band wider apart.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or grip the band closer together.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Plank with Alternating Leg Lift
exercises.description: Assume a plank position, loop band around ankles. Keeping core tight and hips stable, lift one leg slightly, then lower. Alternate legs. Avoid hip rotation.
exercises.easier: Perform plank without leg lifts, or with a lighter band around thighs.
exercises.harder: Hold leg lift for 2-3 seconds, or use a heavier band.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Standing Oblique Crunch
exercises.description: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, loop band around one foot and hold the other end at your temple on the opposite side. Crunch sideways, bringing elbow towards knee. Keep chest open.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform more slowly with a squeeze at the bottom.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Deadlift (RDL)
exercises.description: Stand on the band, feet hip-width, holding handles. Hinge at hips, pushing glutes back, keeping a slight bend in knees and a flat back. Feel stretch in hamstrings. Stand up by squeezing glutes.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or limit the depth of the hinge.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or incorporate a 2-second pause at the bottom.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Kickbacks
exercises.description: Loop band around ankles. Standing tall with a slight lean, kick one leg straight back, squeezing glutes. Control the return. Keep hips stable.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform with the band around the thighs.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or hold the kickback for 2-3 seconds at the top.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Standing Abduction
exercises.description: Loop band around ankles. Stand tall, slowly lift one leg out to the side, maintaining balance and keeping hips forward. Control the movement.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold onto a wall for balance.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or hold the abduction for 2 seconds at the top.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Bird Dog
exercises.description: Start on hands and knees, loop band around one foot and anchor it under the opposite hand. Extend the banded leg straight back and the opposite arm straight forward. Keep core engaged and hips level.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band, or with a lighter band around ankles without hand anchor.
exercises.harder: Hold the extended position for 2-3 seconds.

weeks.description: Slight increase in reps or resistance. Focus on mind-muscle connection and pushing your comfort zone safely.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Chest Press (Floor)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, band wrapped around your upper back, handles in hands. Press hands upwards as if doing a dumbbell press. Keep core tight, avoid arching back.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or wrap it once around your back for less tension.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or pause for 2 seconds at the top of the press.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Seated Row
exercises.description: Sit on the floor with legs extended, loop band around feet. Pull the band handles towards your torso, squeezing shoulder blades. Keep back straight.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or bend your knees slightly.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or hold the contraction for 2 seconds at the top.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Bicep Curl
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding handles with palms up. Curl hands towards shoulders, keeping elbows tucked. Control the eccentric phase.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or stand on the band with only one foot.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform hammer curls (palms facing each other).

exercises.name: Resistance Band Triceps Pushdown (Anchor High)
exercises.description: Anchor band to a high point (door frame, pole). Stand facing it, elbows tucked to sides. Press the band handles downwards, extending arms. Keep core tight.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or step closer to the anchor point.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform with one arm at a time.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Goblet Squat
exercises.description: Stand on the band, hold the handles together at chest level like a goblet. Perform a squat, keeping chest up and elbows inside knees. Drive through heels.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce the depth of the squat.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform a 2-second pause at the bottom.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Hip Thrusts
exercises.description: Sit with upper back against a bench/couch, loop band over hips and stand on it with feet. Drive hips up, squeezing glutes hard at the top. Keep knees pushed out against band.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or use a lighter resistance band.
exercises.harder: Hold the top contraction for 2-3 seconds, or use a heavier band.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Glute Kickback (Kneeling)
exercises.description: On hands and knees, loop band around one foot and the arch of the other foot. Kick the banded leg straight back and up, squeezing glutes. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or hold the kickback for 2 seconds at the top.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Calf Raises
exercises.description: Stand on the band with balls of feet, holding handles for support. Push up onto balls of feet, raising heels. Lower slowly. Maintain balance.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold onto a wall for more support.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform single-leg calf raises.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Thrusters
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding handles at shoulder height. Perform a squat, and as you stand up, explosively press the band overhead. Control descent.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or slow down the explosive movement.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or add a slight jump at the top for more power.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Face Pulls
exercises.muscle_group: Upper Back, Rear Delts, Rotator Cuff
exercises.description: Anchor band to a high point. Grab handles with palms facing each other. Pull the band towards your face, leading with elbows, squeezing shoulder blades. External rotation at the end.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or step closer to the anchor point.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or pause for 2 seconds when band is at your face.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Mountain Climbers
exercises.description: Start in a plank position, loop band around feet/ankles. Drive one knee towards chest, then quickly switch legs. Maintain a flat back and stable hips.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band, or slower tempo.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or increase the speed.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Wood Chop (High to Low)
exercises.description: Anchor band to a high point. Stand sideways, grab handle with both hands. Rotate and pull the band diagonally downwards across your body, engaging obliques. Pivot with back foot.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce the range of motion and weight shift.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform more explosively.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Single Leg RDL
exercises.description: Stand on the band with one foot, holding handles. Hinge at hips, lifting the non-banded leg straight back. Maintain a flat back and balance. Squeeze glutes to return.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or hold onto a wall for balance.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or pause for 2 seconds at the bottom.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Good Mornings
exercises.description: Stand on the band with feet hip-width. Loop the band over your neck/upper traps. Hinge at hips, pushing glutes back, keeping a slight bend in knees and a flat back. Return by squeezing glutes.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or limit the depth of the hinge.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or slow down the eccentric phase.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Clamshells
exercises.description: Lie on your side, knees bent, feet stacked. Loop band above knees. Keeping feet together, lift top knee up, rotating hip open. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or hold the top position for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Bicycle Crunches
exercises.description: Lie on back, loop band around feet/ankles. Bring opposite elbow to opposite knee while extending other leg. Maintain tension on the band. Keep lower back pressed to floor.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or slow down the tempo.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or increase the speed while maintaining form.

weeks.description: Higher reps or sets for endurance, or moving to heavier bands for strength. Focus on sustaining effort.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Chest Press (Floor)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, band wrapped around your upper back, handles in hands. Press hands upwards as if doing a dumbbell press. Keep core tight, avoid arching back.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or wrap it once around your back for less tension.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or pause for 2 seconds at the top of the press.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Seated Row
exercises.description: Sit on the floor with legs extended, loop band around feet. Pull the band handles towards your torso, squeezing shoulder blades. Keep back straight.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or bend your knees slightly.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or hold the contraction for 2 seconds at the top.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Hammer Curls
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding handles with palms facing each other. Curl hands towards shoulders, keeping elbows tucked. Control the eccentric phase.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or stand on the band with only one foot.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform slow, controlled reps.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Triceps Pushdown (Anchor High)
exercises.description: Anchor band to a high point (door frame, pole). Stand facing it, elbows tucked to sides. Press the band handles downwards, extending arms. Keep core tight.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or step closer to the anchor point.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform with one arm at a time.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Overhead Press (Seated)
exercises.description: Sit on the floor, legs extended, loop band under your butt. Hold handles at shoulder height, palms forward. Press the band overhead, extending arms fully. Control descent.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform standing for increased core engagement.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Squats
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding handles at shoulder height. Perform a squat, pushing hips back and down. Drive through heels to stand. Maintain neutral spine.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold the band handles looser.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform a 2-second pause at the bottom of the squat.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Glute Bridge
exercises.description: Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat. Place a loop band above knees. Drive hips up, squeezing glutes. Push knees slightly out against band. Lower slowly.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or use a lighter resistance band.
exercises.harder: Hold the top contraction for 2-3 seconds, or perform single-leg glute bridges.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Lateral Walk (Ankles)
exercises.description: Place a loop band around ankles. Step sideways, maintaining tension on the band. Keep a slight bend in knees, stay low. Repeat for desired reps each side.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or place the band higher up, around the knees.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or take smaller, more controlled steps.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Hamstring Curl (Standing)
exercises.description: Loop band around an anchor point and one ankle. Stand facing the anchor, slightly hinge forward, and curl heel towards glutes. Keep core engaged.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or decrease the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or slowly control the eccentric phase (lowering).

exercises.name: Resistance Band Reverse Lunges
exercises.description: Stand on the band with one foot, holding handles. Step back with the other foot into a lunge, keeping front knee over ankle. Return to start. Alternate legs.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform bodyweight reverse lunges.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform with a pause at the bottom of the lunge.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Thrusters
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding handles at shoulder height. Perform a squat, and as you stand up, explosively press the band overhead. Control descent.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or slow down the explosive movement.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or add a slight jump at the top for more power.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Push-Up
exercises.description: Place band across upper back, looping ends over hands. Perform a push-up, lowering chest to the floor and pressing back up. Keep core tight.
exercises.easier: Perform push-ups on your knees or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Slow the descent to 3 seconds then explode up.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Plank with Alternating Leg Lift
exercises.description: Assume a plank position, loop band around ankles. Keeping core tight and hips stable, lift one leg slightly, then lower. Alternate legs. Avoid hip rotation.
exercises.easier: Perform plank without leg lifts, or with a lighter band around thighs.
exercises.harder: Hold leg lift for 2-3 seconds, or use a heavier band.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Standing Oblique Crunch
exercises.description: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, loop band around one foot and hold the other end at your temple on the opposite side. Crunch sideways, bringing elbow towards knee. Keep chest open.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform more slowly with a squeeze at the bottom.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Jumping Jacks
exercises.description: Stand upright, loop a light band around your ankles or just above knees. Perform jumping jacks, pushing against the band resistance. Keep core engaged.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform step jacks with the band.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or add a small hop at the top of each leg abduction.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Deadlift (RDL)
exercises.description: Stand on the band, feet hip-width, holding handles. Hinge at hips, pushing glutes back, keeping a slight bend in knees and a flat back. Feel stretch in hamstrings. Stand up by squeezing glutes.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or limit the depth of the hinge.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or incorporate a 2-second pause at the bottom.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Kickbacks (Glute Emphasis)
exercises.description: Loop band around ankles. Standing tall with a slight lean, kick one leg straight back, squeezing glutes. Control the return. Keep hips stable.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform with the band around the thighs.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or hold the kickback for 2-3 seconds at the top.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Standing Abduction
exercises.description: Loop band around ankles. Stand tall, slowly lift one leg out to the side, maintaining balance and keeping hips forward. Control the movement.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold onto a wall for balance.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or hold the abduction for 2 seconds at the top.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Side Plank with Hip Abduction
exercises.description: Assume a side plank position, loop band around ankles. Keeping core tight and hips stable, lift the top leg up against the band. Lower slowly. Repeat per side.
exercises.easier: Perform side plank without abduction, or with band around knees.
exercises.harder: Hold the abducted leg for 2-3 seconds at the top.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Leg Extensions (Seated)
exercises.description: Sit on a chair, loop band around one ankle and secure the other end under the chair leg. Extend leg straight out, squeezing quads. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform with less range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or pause for 2 seconds at the top.

weeks.description: Final push for the month. Focus on explosive movements and maintaining strength, or increasing band tension where possible.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Chest Press (Floor)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, band wrapped around your upper back, handles in hands. Press hands upwards as if doing a dumbbell press. Keep core tight, avoid arching back.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or wrap it once around your back for less tension.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or pause for 2 seconds at the top of the press.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Seated Row
exercises.description: Sit on the floor with legs extended, loop band around feet. Pull the band handles towards your torso, squeezing shoulder blades. Keep back straight.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or bend your knees slightly.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or hold the contraction for 2 seconds at the top.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Bicep Curl (Supinated)
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding handles with palms up. Curl hands towards shoulders, keeping elbows tucked. Control the eccentric phase.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or stand on the band with only one foot.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform slow, controlled reps with a peak contraction.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Triceps Overhead Extension (Single Arm)
exercises.description: Anchor band under one foot, hold handle with one hand behind head. Extend arm overhead, keeping elbow close to ear. Control the movement.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform with two hands.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or add a pause at the top of the extension.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Lateral Raises
exercises.description: Stand on the band, crossing it under your feet for more tension. Hold handles at your sides. Lift arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in elbows, up to shoulder height. Control descent.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or do not cross the band under your feet.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or increase the time under tension with slower reps.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Squats (Heavy Band)
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding handles at shoulder height. Perform a squat, pushing hips back and down. Drive through heels to stand. Maintain neutral spine.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold the band handles looser.
exercises.harder: Use the heaviest band available and control the full range of motion.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Single Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.description: Lie on back, one knee bent, foot flat, other leg extended. Place a loop band above bent knee. Drive hips up, squeezing glutes. Keep extended leg in line. Lower slowly. Repeat per side.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or use a lighter resistance band.
exercises.harder: Hold the top contraction for 2-3 seconds, or use a heavier band.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Band Walk (Diagonal)
exercises.description: Place a loop band around ankles. Step diagonally forward, maintaining tension on the band. Keep a slight bend in knees, stay low. Repeat for desired reps each side.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or place the band higher up, around the thighs.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band and take controlled, deliberate steps.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Hamstring Curl (Lying)
exercises.description: Lie on stomach, loop band around ankles. Anchor the other end to a sturdy object. Curl heels towards glutes, squeezing hamstrings. Control the eccentric phase.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform with a 2-second hold at peak contraction.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Romanian Deadlifts (Heavy Band)
exercises.description: Stand on the band, feet hip-width, holding handles. Hinge at hips, pushing glutes back, keeping a slight bend in knees and a flat back. Feel stretch in hamstrings. Stand up by squeezing glutes.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or limit the depth of the hinge.
exercises.harder: Use the heaviest band available and focus on the glute squeeze.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Squat Jumps
exercises.description: Stand on a loop band with feet shoulder-width, holding the band at shoulder height (or loop above knees). Perform a squat and explode upwards into a jump. Land softly.
exercises.easier: Perform regular resistance band squats without the jump.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or increase the height and explosiveness of the jump.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Renegade Row (Plank)
exercises.description: Assume a plank position, hands gripping band handles, band anchored under the other hand. Row one handle up towards your chest, keeping hips square. Alternate sides.
exercises.easier: Perform from knees or without a band (just plank with rows).
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band and minimize hip sway.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Bicycle Crunches
exercises.description: Lie on back, loop band around feet/ankles. Bring opposite elbow to opposite knee while extending other leg. Maintain tension on the band. Keep lower back pressed to floor.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or slow down the tempo.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or increase the speed while maintaining form.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Anti-Rotation Press (Pallof Press)
exercises.description: Anchor band to a side at chest height. Stand perpendicular to anchor, holding band with both hands at chest. Press band straight out, resisting rotation. Hold for 1-2 seconds, then return. Repeat per side.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or stand closer to the anchor point.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or stand further from the anchor point.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Jumping Jacks
exercises.description: Stand upright, loop a light band around your ankles or just above knees. Perform jumping jacks, pushing against the band resistance. Keep core engaged.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform step jacks with the band.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or increase the speed and explosiveness.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Deadlift (Heavy Band)
exercises.description: Stand on the band, feet hip-width, holding handles. Hinge at hips, pushing glutes back, keeping a slight bend in knees and a flat back. Feel stretch in hamstrings. Stand up by squeezing glutes.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or limit the depth of the hinge.
exercises.harder: Use the heaviest band available and perform with strict form.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Glute Kickback (Standing, Heavy Band)
exercises.description: Loop band around ankles. Standing tall with a slight lean, kick one leg straight back, squeezing glutes. Control the return. Keep hips stable.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform with the band around the thighs.
exercises.harder: Use the heaviest band available and hold the kickback for 2-3 seconds at the top.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Fire Hydrants
exercises.description: Start on hands and knees, loop band above knees. Keeping knee bent, lift one leg out to the side like a dog. Keep hips level and core engaged. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or hold the top position for 2 seconds.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Bird Dog (Advanced)
exercises.description: Start on hands and knees, loop band around one foot and anchor it under the opposite hand. Extend the banded leg straight back and the opposite arm straight forward. Keep core engaged and hips level.
exercises.easier: Perform without a band, or with a lighter band around ankles without hand anchor.
exercises.harder: Hold the extended position for 3-5 seconds and minimize wobble.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Standing Leg Curls
exercises.description: Loop band around an anchor point and one ankle. Stand facing the anchor, slightly hinge forward, and curl heel towards glutes. Keep core engaged.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or decrease the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform with a 2-second squeeze at the top.

plan_data.plan_name: 4-Week Banded Full Body for Weight Loss & Muscle Building (Intermediate)
plan_data.overview: A progressive 4-week workout plan for intermediate individuals, focusing on weight loss and muscle building using resistance bands. The plan emphasizes full body, glutes, legs, back, and core, with careful consideration to avoid lower back strain. Progressive overload is applied across the weeks to enhance strength and endurance.

weeks.description: Establish proper form and build foundational strength with resistance bands. Focus on muscle activation and controlled movements.

exercises.name: Band Push-Ups
exercises.description: Place a resistance band across your upper back, holding ends in your hands under your palms. Perform a push-up, pushing against the band's resistance. Keep core tight, body in a straight line.
exercises.easier: Perform push-ups from your knees with the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or elevate your feet.

exercises.name: Band Squats
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding ends at shoulder height. Descend into a squat, pushing hips back and keeping chest up. Drive through heels to stand, squeezing glutes.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform bodyweight squats.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or add a 2-second pause at the bottom.

exercises.name: Band Overhead Press
exercises.description: Stand on the band with one or both feet, holding the ends. Press the band overhead, extending arms fully. Control the eccentric phase.
exercises.easier: Kneel to shorten the band or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform in a half-kneeling position for core engagement.

exercises.name: Band Glute Bridges
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat. Place a band around your knees. Lift hips off the floor, squeezing glutes, keeping knees pushed out against the band. Lower slowly.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band.
exercises.harder: Pause for 2-3 seconds at the top of the bridge.

exercises.name: Band Rows
exercises.description: Sit on the floor with legs extended, band looped around feet. Hold ends, pull elbows back, squeezing shoulder blades together. Control release.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or decrease range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform one arm at a time.

exercises.name: Band Lunges
exercises.description: Step one foot onto the band, hold ends. Step back with the other foot into a lunge. Lower until both knees are at 90 degrees. Drive up through the front heel.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band or hold onto support.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or elevate your front foot.

exercises.name: Band Bicep Curls
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding ends with an underhand grip. Curl arms up, squeezing biceps. Control the eccentric phase.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or shorten the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or perform one arm at a time.

exercises.name: Banded Side Planks
exercises.description: Loop a band around your knees. Get into a side plank position, supporting on forearm. Lift your top leg against the band's resistance, keeping hips stable. Hold briefly, then lower.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band or from your knees.
exercises.harder: Extend your top arm overhead during the lift.

exercises.name: Band Romanian Deadlifts (RDLs)
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding ends. Keeping a slight bend in knees, hinge at hips, lowering torso until a stretch is felt in hamstrings. Squeeze glutes to return to stand.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform without a band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or perform single-leg RDLs.

exercises.name: Banded Lateral Walks
exercises.description: Place a mini-band around your ankles or just above knees. Get into a slight squat. Step sideways, maintaining tension on the band. Take equal steps in both directions.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or place it higher on the thighs.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or get into a deeper squat.

exercises.name: Band Leg Press (lying)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent. Loop a band around your feet and hold the ends with your hands. Extend legs, pressing feet against the band's resistance. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or focus on one leg at a time.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or add a pulse at the top.

exercises.name: Bird-Dog with Band
exercises.description: Start on hands and knees. Place a mini-band around your feet. Extend opposite arm and leg, pushing out against the band. Keep core tight, avoid arching lower back. Alternate sides.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band.
exercises.harder: Hold the extension for 3 seconds.

exercises.name: Band Jumping Jacks
exercises.description: Place a mini-band around your ankles. Perform jumping jacks, pushing out against the band's resistance as you spread your legs. Maintain controlled movement.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band or do step jacks.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or add a squat at the bottom.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Aparts
exercises.description: Hold a resistance band with both hands, arms extended in front at shoulder height. Pull the band apart, squeezing shoulder blades together, until band touches chest. Control return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold closer to the ends.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or hold for a 2-second squeeze.

exercises.name: Band Mountain Climbers
exercises.description: Place a mini-band around your feet or ankles. Get into a plank position. Drive knees towards chest alternately, maintaining core stability and pushing against band resistance.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band or at a slower pace.
exercises.harder: Increase speed or use a stronger band.

exercises.name: Band Pallof Press
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band to a stable object at chest height. Stand perpendicular to the anchor, holding the band with both hands. Press the band straight out from your chest, resisting rotation. Control return. Perform on both sides.
exercises.easier: Step closer to the anchor point or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Step further from the anchor or perform from a kneeling position.

weeks.description: Increase reps and/or sets to challenge muscles further. Focus on mind-muscle connection and controlled power.

exercises.name: Banded Side Planks
exercises.description: Loop a band around your knees. Get into a side plank position, supporting on forearm. Lift your top leg against the band's resistance, keeping hips stable. Hold briefly, then lower.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band or from your knees.
exercises.harder: Extend your top arm overhead during the lift.

exercises.name: Band Romanian Deadlifts (RDLs)
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding ends. Keeping a slight bend in knees, hinge at hips, lowering torso until a stretch is felt in hamstrings. Squeeze glutes to return to stand.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform without a band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or perform single-leg RDLs.

exercises.name: Banded Lateral Walks
exercises.description: Place a mini-band around your ankles or just above knees. Get into a slight squat. Step sideways, maintaining tension on the band. Take equal steps in both directions.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or place it higher on the thighs.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or get into a deeper squat.

exercises.name: Band Leg Press (lying)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent. Loop a band around your feet and hold the ends with your hands. Extend legs, pressing feet against the band's resistance. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or focus on one leg at a time.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or add a pulse at the top.

exercises.name: Bird-Dog with Band
exercises.description: Start on hands and knees. Place a mini-band around your feet. Extend opposite arm and leg, pushing out against the band. Keep core tight, avoid arching lower back. Alternate sides.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band.
exercises.harder: Hold the extension for 3 seconds.

exercises.name: Band Jumping Jacks
exercises.description: Place a mini-band around your ankles. Perform jumping jacks, pushing out against the band's resistance as you spread your legs. Maintain controlled movement.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band or do step jacks.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or add a squat at the bottom.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Aparts
exercises.description: Hold a resistance band with both hands, arms extended in front at shoulder height. Pull the band apart, squeezing shoulder blades together, until band touches chest. Control return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold closer to the ends.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or hold for a 2-second squeeze.

exercises.name: Band Mountain Climbers
exercises.description: Place a mini-band around your feet or ankles. Get into a plank position. Drive knees towards chest alternately, maintaining core stability and pushing against band resistance.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band or at a slower pace.
exercises.harder: Increase speed or use a stronger band.

exercises.name: Band Pallof Press
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band to a stable object at chest height. Stand perpendicular to the anchor, holding the band with both hands. Press the band straight out from your chest, resisting rotation. Control return. Perform on both sides.
exercises.easier: Step closer to the anchor point or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Step further from the anchor or perform from a kneeling position.

weeks.description: Push your limits with the heaviest bands you can manage for the prescribed reps. Focus on perfecting form and maximizing muscle recruitment.

exercises.name: Band Push-Ups
exercises.description: Place a resistance band across your upper back, holding ends in your hands under your palms. Perform a push-up, pushing against the band's resistance. Keep core tight, body in a straight line.
exercises.easier: Perform push-ups from your knees with the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger resistance band or elevate your feet.

exercises.name: Band Squats
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding ends at shoulder height. Descend into a squat, pushing hips back and keeping chest up. Drive through heels to stand, squeezing glutes.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform bodyweight squats.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or add a 2-second pause at the bottom.

exercises.name: Band Overhead Press
exercises.description: Stand on the band with one or both feet, holding the ends. Press the band overhead, extending arms fully. Control the eccentric phase.
exercises.easier: Kneel to shorten the band or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform in a half-kneeling position for core engagement.

exercises.name: Band Glute Bridges
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat. Place a band around your knees. Lift hips off the floor, squeezing glutes, keeping knees pushed out against the band. Lower slowly.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band.
exercises.harder: Pause for 2-3 seconds at the top of the bridge.

exercises.name: Band Rows
exercises.description: Sit on the floor with legs extended, band looped around feet. Hold ends, pull elbows back, squeezing shoulder blades together. Control release.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or decrease range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a heavier band or perform one arm at a time.

exercises.name: Band Lunges
exercises.description: Step one foot onto the band, hold ends. Step back with the other foot into a lunge. Lower until both knees are at 90 degrees. Drive up through the front heel.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band or hold onto support.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or elevate your front foot.

exercises.name: Band Bicep Curls
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding ends with an underhand grip. Curl arms up, squeezing biceps. Control the eccentric phase.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or shorten the range of motion.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or perform one arm at a time.

exercises.name: Banded Side Planks
exercises.description: Loop a band around your knees. Get into a side plank position, supporting on forearm. Lift your top leg against the band's resistance, keeping hips stable. Hold briefly, then lower.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band or from your knees.
exercises.harder: Extend your top arm overhead during the lift.

exercises.name: Band Romanian Deadlifts (RDLs)
exercises.description: Stand on the band, holding ends. Keeping a slight bend in knees, hinge at hips, lowering torso until a stretch is felt in hamstrings. Squeeze glutes to return to stand.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform without a band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or perform single-leg RDLs.

exercises.name: Banded Lateral Walks
exercises.description: Place a mini-band around your ankles or just above knees. Get into a slight squat. Step sideways, maintaining tension on the band. Take equal steps in both directions.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or place it higher on the thighs.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or get into a deeper squat.

exercises.name: Band Leg Press (lying)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent. Loop a band around your feet and hold the ends with your hands. Extend legs, pressing feet against the band's resistance. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or focus on one leg at a time.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or add a pulse at the top.

exercises.name: Bird-Dog with Band
exercises.description: Start on hands and knees. Place a mini-band around your feet. Extend opposite arm and leg, pushing out against the band. Keep core tight, avoid arching lower back. Alternate sides.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band.
exercises.harder: Hold the extension for 3 seconds.

exercises.name: Band Jumping Jacks
exercises.description: Place a mini-band around your ankles. Perform jumping jacks, pushing out against the band's resistance as you spread your legs. Maintain controlled movement.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band or do step jacks.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or add a squat at the bottom.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Aparts
exercises.description: Hold a resistance band with both hands, arms extended in front at shoulder height. Pull the band apart, squeezing shoulder blades together, until band touches chest. Control return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold closer to the ends.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or hold for a 2-second squeeze.

exercises.name: Band Mountain Climbers
exercises.description: Place a mini-band around your feet or ankles. Get into a plank position. Drive knees towards chest alternately, maintaining core stability and pushing against band resistance.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band or at a slower pace.
exercises.harder: Increase speed or use a stronger band.

exercises.name: Band Pallof Press
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band to a stable object at chest height. Stand perpendicular to the anchor, holding the band with both hands. Press the band straight out from your chest, resisting rotation. Control return. Perform on both sides.
exercises.easier: Step closer to the anchor point or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Step further from the anchor or perform from a kneeling position.

plan_data.overview: An intermediate 4-week progressive plan designed for weight loss and muscle building, utilizing resistance bands for a full-body challenge while protecting the lower back. Focuses on glutes, legs, back, and core.

weeks.description: Establish proper form and mind-muscle connection with resistance bands. Focus on controlled movements and activating target muscles.

exercises.name: Band Squats
exercises.description: Place a resistance band just above your knees. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out. Push hips back and bend knees, lowering until thighs are parallel to floor, pushing knees out against the band. Drive through heels to stand.
exercises.easier: Perform bodyweight squats without the band or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or add a 2-second pause at the bottom of the squat.

exercises.name: Band Push-Ups
exercises.description: Loop a resistance band across your upper back, holding ends in each hand under your palms. Start in plank position. Lower your chest to the floor, keeping elbows at 45 degrees. Push back up against the band's resistance. Keep core tight.
exercises.easier: Perform push-ups on your knees with or without the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or elevate your feet on a stable surface.

exercises.name: Band Glute Bridges
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat on floor close to glutes. Place a resistance band just above your knees. Drive hips up towards the ceiling, squeezing glutes at the top and pushing knees out against the band. Slowly lower down.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band, or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Perform single-leg glute bridges with the band.

exercises.name: Plank with Band March
exercises.description: Start in a high plank position with a resistance band looped around your feet/ankles. Keeping core tight and hips stable, alternate lifting one foot a few inches off the ground, engaging glutes. Avoid rocking your hips.
exercises.easier: Hold a standard plank or perform without the band.
exercises.harder: Increase the height you lift your leg, or hold the lifted leg for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Walks
exercises.description: Place a resistance band around your ankles or just above your knees. Get into a slight squat stance. Take controlled steps sideways, keeping tension on the band. Take 10 steps in one direction, then 10 in the other. (1 rep = 10 steps each side).
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or place the band higher up (above knees).
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or take longer, slower steps.

exercises.name: Band Rows (Bent-Over)
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band, feet shoulder-width apart. Hinge at hips, maintaining a flat back. Hold band ends with palms facing each other. Pull the band up towards your chest, squeezing shoulder blades. Control the release.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold the band closer to the ends.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or pull with a 2-second squeeze at the top.

exercises.name: Band Lunges (Alternating)
exercises.description: Loop a resistance band under one foot and hold the other end in the opposite hand (e.g., band under left foot, hold in right hand). Step forward with the banded foot into a lunge, lowering until both knees are at 90 degrees. Push back up. Alternate legs.
exercises.easier: Perform bodyweight lunges or use a lighter band/less resistance.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or increase the range of motion.

exercises.name: Bird-Dog with Band
exercises.description: Start on all fours. Loop a resistance band around one foot and hold the other end in the opposite hand. Extend the banded leg straight back while simultaneously extending the opposite arm forward. Keep core engaged and hips stable. Return and alternate sides.
exercises.easier: Perform standard bird-dog without the band.
exercises.harder: Hold the extended position for 2-3 seconds, focusing on stability.

exercises.name: Band Overhead Press
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the ends of the band at shoulder height, palms facing forward. Press the band straight overhead until arms are fully extended. Control the descent.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform seated.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or add a 2-second pause at the top.

exercises.name: Band Chest Press (Standing)
exercises.description: Loop a long resistance band around a sturdy anchor point (e.g., pole, door anchor) at chest height. Stand facing away from the anchor, holding ends of the band in each hand at chest level, elbows bent. Press hands straight forward, extending arms, then slowly return.
exercises.easier: Step closer to the anchor point to reduce tension.
exercises.harder: Step further from the anchor point to increase tension.

exercises.name: Band Bicep Curls
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band with feet hip-width apart. Hold the ends of the band, palms facing forward. Keep elbows tucked to sides and curl the band up towards your shoulders. Squeeze at the top, then slowly lower.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or widen your stance on the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or narrow your stance on the band for more tension.

exercises.name: Band Triceps Extensions (Overhead)
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band with one foot. Hold the other end of the band with both hands behind your head, elbows pointing forward. Extend arms overhead, straightening elbows. Control the return to starting position.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold the band closer to the anchored end.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or move your foot further onto the band.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Aparts
exercises.description: Hold a light resistance band with both hands, palms down, arms extended in front of you at shoulder height. Keeping arms straight, pull the band apart, squeezing shoulder blades together until the band touches your chest. Slowly return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold the band wider apart.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or slow down the eccentric (return) phase.

exercises.name: Band Adductions (Standing)
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band to a sturdy object. Loop the other end around your inner ankle (leg farthest from anchor). Stand tall, slight bend in knees. Pull the banded leg across your body, squeezing inner thigh. Control the return. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or step closer to the anchor.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or step further from the anchor.

exercises.name: Band Abductions (Standing)
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band to a sturdy object. Loop the other end around your outer ankle (leg closest to anchor). Stand tall, slight bend in knees. Lift the banded leg out to the side, squeezing glute and outer thigh. Control the return. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or step closer to the anchor.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or step further from the anchor.

exercises.name: Dead Bug with Band
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent at 90 degrees over hips, arms extended up. Loop a small resistance band around your feet. Keep lower back pressed into the floor. Slowly extend one leg straight out while simultaneously lowering the opposite arm overhead. Return and alternate. Maintain core engagement and band tension.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band, focusing on form.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or slow down the movement for increased time under tension.

weeks.description: Increase volume slightly to challenge muscles further. Concentrate on explosive yet controlled movements.

exercises.name: Band Squats
exercises.description: Place a resistance band just above your knees. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out. Push hips back and bend knees, lowering until thighs are parallel to floor, pushing knees out against the band. Drive through heels to stand.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce reps.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or add a slight jump at the top (banded squat jumps).

exercises.name: Band Push-Ups
exercises.description: Loop a resistance band across your upper back, holding ends in each hand under your palms. Start in plank position. Lower your chest to the floor, keeping elbows at 45 degrees. Push back up against the band's resistance. Keep core tight.
exercises.easier: Perform push-ups on your knees with or without the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or slow the descent (eccentric) phase to 3 seconds.

exercises.name: Band Glute Bridges
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat on floor close to glutes. Place a resistance band just above your knees. Drive hips up towards the ceiling, squeezing glutes at the top and pushing knees out against the band. Slowly lower down.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band, or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Perform single-leg glute bridges with the band, or add a 2-second hold at the top.

exercises.name: Plank with Band March
exercises.description: Start in a high plank position with a resistance band looped around your feet/ankles. Keeping core tight and hips stable, alternate lifting one foot a few inches off the ground, engaging glutes. Avoid rocking your hips.
exercises.easier: Hold a standard plank or perform without the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or hold each lifted leg for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Walks
exercises.description: Place a resistance band around your ankles or just above your knees. Get into a slight squat stance. Take controlled steps sideways, keeping tension on the band. Take 12 steps in one direction, then 12 in the other. (1 rep = 12 steps each side).
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or place the band higher up (above knees).
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or take longer, slower steps with more resistance.

exercises.name: Band Rows (Bent-Over)
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band, feet shoulder-width apart. Hinge at hips, maintaining a flat back. Hold band ends with palms facing each other. Pull the band up towards your chest, squeezing shoulder blades. Control the release.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold the band closer to the ends.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or pull with a 2-second squeeze at the top.

exercises.name: Band Lunges (Alternating)
exercises.description: Loop a resistance band under one foot and hold the other end in the opposite hand. Step forward with the banded foot into a lunge, lowering until both knees are at 90 degrees. Push back up. Alternate legs.
exercises.easier: Perform bodyweight lunges or use a lighter band/less resistance.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or increase the speed of the concentric (push up) phase.

exercises.name: Bird-Dog with Band
exercises.description: Start on all fours. Loop a resistance band around one foot and hold the other end in the opposite hand. Extend the banded leg straight back while simultaneously extending the opposite arm forward. Keep core engaged and hips stable. Return and alternate sides.
exercises.easier: Perform standard bird-dog without the band.
exercises.harder: Hold the extended position for 3-4 seconds, focusing on stability and challenging the band.

exercises.name: Band Overhead Press
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the ends of the band at shoulder height, palms facing forward. Press the band straight overhead until arms are fully extended. Control the descent.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform seated.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band and perform with a slight pause at the bottom.

exercises.name: Band Chest Press (Standing)
exercises.description: Loop a long resistance band around a sturdy anchor point at chest height. Stand facing away from the anchor, holding ends of the band in each hand at chest level, elbows bent. Press hands straight forward, extending arms, then slowly return.
exercises.easier: Step closer to the anchor point to reduce tension.
exercises.harder: Step further from the anchor point, or perform one arm at a time for greater challenge.

exercises.name: Band Bicep Curls
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band with feet hip-width apart. Hold the ends of the band, palms facing forward. Keep elbows tucked to sides and curl the band up towards your shoulders. Squeeze at the top, then slowly lower.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or widen your stance on the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or narrow your stance on the band for more tension, focusing on the squeeze.

exercises.name: Band Triceps Extensions (Overhead)
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band with one foot. Hold the other end of the band with both hands behind your head, elbows pointing forward. Extend arms overhead, straightening elbows. Control the return to starting position.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold the band closer to the anchored end.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or move your foot further onto the band, focusing on a deep stretch and powerful extension.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Aparts
exercises.description: Hold a light resistance band with both hands, palms down, arms extended in front of you at shoulder height. Keeping arms straight, pull the band apart, squeezing shoulder blades together until the band touches your chest. Slowly return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold the band wider apart.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or add a 2-second hold at the fully pulled apart position.

exercises.name: Band Adductions (Standing)
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band to a sturdy object. Loop the other end around your inner ankle (leg farthest from anchor). Stand tall, slight bend in knees. Pull the banded leg across your body, squeezing inner thigh. Control the return. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or step closer to the anchor.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or step further from the anchor, focusing on a slower, controlled return.

exercises.name: Band Abductions (Standing)
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band to a sturdy object. Loop the other end around your outer ankle (leg closest to anchor). Stand tall, slight bend in knees. Lift the banded leg out to the side, squeezing glute and outer thigh. Control the return. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or step closer to the anchor.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or step further from the anchor, focusing on the glute contraction at the top.

exercises.name: Dead Bug with Band
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent at 90 degrees over hips, arms extended up. Loop a small resistance band around your feet. Keep lower back pressed into the floor. Slowly extend one leg straight out while simultaneously lowering the opposite arm overhead. Return and alternate. Maintain core engagement and band tension.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band, focusing on form.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, and extend both legs simultaneously while keeping back flat (advanced).

weeks.description: Increase sets and reps where appropriate, focusing on maintaining perfect form under increased fatigue. Incorporate more challenging variations.

exercises.name: Band Squats
exercises.description: Place a resistance band just above your knees. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out. Push hips back and bend knees, lowering until thighs are parallel to floor, pushing knees out against the band. Drive through heels to stand.
exercises.easier: Reduce to 3 sets or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or add a pulse at the bottom of the squat.

exercises.name: Band Push-Ups
exercises.description: Loop a resistance band across your upper back, holding ends in each hand under your palms. Start in plank position. Lower your chest to the floor, keeping elbows at 45 degrees. Push back up against the band's resistance. Keep core tight.
exercises.easier: Reduce to 3 sets or perform push-ups on your knees with or without the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or elevate your feet on a stable surface for decline push-ups.

exercises.name: Band Glute Bridges (Single-Leg)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, one foot flat on floor. Loop a resistance band above your knee. Extend the other leg straight up or out. Drive hips up, squeezing glutes and pushing knee out against band. Slowly lower. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform standard two-leg glute bridges with the band.
exercises.harder: Add a 3-second hold at the top of the single-leg bridge.

exercises.name: Banded Side Planks with Hip Dip
exercises.description: Start in a side plank position, supporting on forearm. Place a resistance band around your ankles. Keeping body in a straight line, slowly lower your hips towards the floor, then lift them back up, pushing top leg against the band. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Hold a standard side plank or perform without the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or add a leg lift at the top of the hip dip.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Walks
exercises.description: Place a resistance band around your ankles or just above your knees. Get into a slight squat stance. Take controlled steps sideways, keeping tension on the band. Take 10 steps in one direction, then 10 in the other. (1 rep = 10 steps each side).
exercises.easier: Reduce to 3 sets or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or perform with a lower, more athletic stance.

exercises.name: Band Rows (Bent-Over)
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band, feet shoulder-width apart. Hinge at hips, maintaining a flat back. Hold band ends with palms facing each other. Pull the band up towards your chest, squeezing shoulder blades. Control the release.
exercises.easier: Reduce to 3 sets or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or perform with a 3-second eccentric (lowering) phase.

exercises.name: Band Reverse Lunges (Alternating)
exercises.description: Loop a resistance band under one foot and hold the other end in the opposite hand. Step *backward* with the banded foot into a lunge, lowering until both knees are at 90 degrees. Push back up to start. Alternate legs.
exercises.easier: Perform bodyweight reverse lunges or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or add a pulse at the bottom of the lunge.

exercises.name: Band Donkey Kicks
exercises.description: Start on all fours. Place a small loop band around one foot and anchor the other end under the opposite knee (or use ankle band). Keep knee bent and push the banded foot straight up towards the ceiling, squeezing glute. Control the return. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or add a 2-second hold at the top of the kick.

exercises.harder: Use a stronger band and perform in a half-kneeling position for core stability.

exercises.harder: Step further from the anchor point, or perform with a 2-second squeeze at the peak contraction.

exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or widen your stance on the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band and perform with a 3-second eccentric (lowering) phase.

exercises.name: Band Triceps Extensions (Overhead)

exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or hold the band closer to the anchored end.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or move your foot further onto the band, focusing on a deep stretch and powerful extension.

exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or perform with palms facing up for a bicep/upper back variation.

exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band to a sturdy object. Loop the other end around your inner ankle (leg farthest from anchor). Stand tall, slight bend in knees. Pull the banded leg across your body, squeezing inner thigh. Control the return. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Reduce to 3 sets or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or stand further from the anchor, increasing the range of motion.

exercises.name: Band Abductions (Standing)
exercises.description: Anchor a resistance band to a sturdy object. Loop the other end around your outer ankle (leg closest to anchor). Stand tall, slight bend in knees. Lift the banded leg out to the side, squeezing glute and outer thigh. Control the return. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Reduce to 3 sets or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band or stand further from the anchor, focusing on maximal glute activation.

exercises.name: Dead Bug with Band
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent at 90 degrees over hips, arms extended up. Loop a small resistance band around your feet. Keep lower back pressed into the floor. Slowly extend one leg straight out while simultaneously lowering the opposite arm overhead. Return and alternate. Maintain core engagement and band tension.
exercises.easier: Reduce to 3 sets or perform without the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or extend both legs simultaneously while keeping back flat (advanced).

weeks.description: Push to your limits with higher volume and challenging variations. Focus on perfect execution even with increased intensity.

exercises.name: Band Squats (Pulse at Bottom)
exercises.description: Place a resistance band just above your knees. Perform a squat, and at the bottom, perform a small pulse (move up and down a few inches) before standing up fully. Focus on consistent tension on the band.
exercises.easier: Perform regular banded squats without the pulse.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or add a full jump at the top after the pulse.

exercises.name: Band Push-Ups (Decline)
exercises.description: Loop a resistance band across your upper back. Place feet on an elevated surface (e.g., chair, step) for a decline position. Perform push-ups, lowering chest towards the floor and pushing back up against the band's resistance.
exercises.easier: Perform regular banded push-ups or on knees.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or perform with a slower 3-second eccentric phase.

exercises.name: Band Glute Bridges (Single-Leg)
exercises.description: Lie on your back, knees bent, one foot flat on floor. Loop a resistance band above your knee. Extend the other leg straight up or out. Drive hips up, squeezing glutes and pushing knee out against band. Slowly lower. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform standard two-leg glute bridges with the band.
exercises.harder: Add a 3-second hold at the top of the single-leg bridge, or wear a stronger band.

exercises.name: Banded Side Planks with Hip Dip & Leg Lift
exercises.description: Start in a side plank. Place a resistance band around your ankles. Lower hips, then lift them while simultaneously lifting the top leg. Control the movement. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform just side plank hip dips or hold a standard side plank.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or slow down the entire movement for increased time under tension.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Walks (Lower Stance)
exercises.description: Place a strong resistance band around your ankles or just above your knees. Get into a deep squat stance (hips lower than Week 3). Take controlled steps sideways, maintaining the low stance and constant tension on the band. Take 15 steps in one direction, then 15 in the other. (1 rep = 15 steps each side).
exercises.easier: Reduce reps or use a lighter band and/or higher stance.
exercises.harder: Use an even stronger band, or increase steps per side.

exercises.name: Band Rows (Bent-Over with Hold)
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band, feet shoulder-width apart. Hinge at hips, maintaining a flat back. Hold band ends. Pull the band up towards your chest, squeezing shoulder blades, and hold the contraction for 2 seconds. Control the release.
exercises.easier: Reduce hold time or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or add a 3-second eccentric (lowering) phase after the hold.

exercises.name: Band Bulgarian Split Squats
exercises.description: Place a resistance band under your front foot and hold the ends in your hands. Elevate your rear foot on a low, stable surface (e.g., step). Lower into a lunge, keeping front knee behind toes. Push back up. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform regular banded reverse lunges or bodyweight split squats.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or add a 2-second pause at the bottom.

exercises.name: Band Fire Hydrants
exercises.description: Start on all fours with a loop resistance band just above your knees. Keeping knee bent at 90 degrees, lift one leg out to the side like a dog peeing on a hydrant, pushing against the band. Keep core tight and hips stable. Control the return. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or add a 2-second hold at the top of the lift.

exercises.name: Band Overhead Press (Half-Kneeling)
exercises.description: Kneel on one knee, with the long resistance band anchored under the front foot. Hold the band ends at shoulder height. Press the band straight overhead, focusing on core stability to prevent rocking. Control the descent. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform standing banded overhead press.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or perform with a 2-second pause at the top.

exercises.name: Band Chest Press (Single Arm)
exercises.description: Loop a long resistance band around a sturdy anchor point at chest height. Stand perpendicular to the anchor, holding one end of the band in the hand farthest from the anchor. Press straight forward, extending arm, then slowly return. Engage core to prevent rotation. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform two-hand banded chest press or step closer to anchor.
exercises.harder: Step further from the anchor, or perform with a 2-second squeeze at peak contraction.

exercises.name: Band Bicep Curls (Iso-Hold)
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band. Curl one arm up to the halfway point and hold it. Then perform full curls with the other arm. After reps, switch roles. This creates an isometric hold on one side while the other works.
exercises.easier: Perform regular banded bicep curls.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or extend the isometric hold duration.

exercises.name: Band Triceps Kickbacks
exercises.description: Stand on one end of a long resistance band. Hinge at hips, maintaining a flat back, elbow tucked at your side. Extend your arm straight back, squeezing triceps. Control the return. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or reduce reps.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or add a 2-second squeeze at the fully extended position.

exercises.name: Band Face Pulls
exercises.description: Anchor a long resistance band at eye level. Grab the band with an overhand grip, thumbs pointing towards you. Step back to create tension. Pull the band towards your face, flaring elbows out and squeezing shoulder blades. Control the return.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or step closer to the anchor.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or add a 2-second hold at the peak contraction.

exercises.name: Band Good Mornings
exercises.description: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band, holding the ends behind your neck/shoulders. Keep a slight bend in knees and hinge at hips, pushing glutes back and maintaining a flat back. Lower until you feel a hamstring stretch, then drive through heels to return. Focus on hip hinge, not spinal flexion.
exercises.easier: Use a lighter band or perform bodyweight good mornings.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or slow the eccentric (lowering) phase to 3 seconds.

exercises.name: Band Clamshells
exercises.description: Lie on your side with knees bent at 90 degrees, feet stacked. Place a loop resistance band just above your knees. Keep feet together and lift your top knee, externally rotating your hip and pushing against the band. Slowly lower. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Perform without the band.
exercises.harder: Use a stronger band, or add a pulse at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Band Pallof Press (Standing)
exercises.description: Anchor a long resistance band at chest height. Stand perpendicular to the anchor, holding the band with both hands at your chest. Step out to create tension. Press the band straight out in front of you, resisting the band's pull to rotate your torso. Hold briefly, then slowly return. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
exercises.easier: Step closer to the anchor point, or use a lighter band.
exercises.harder: Step further from the anchor point, or perform in a half-kneeling position for greater challenge.
